programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Randy thwarts Steve's efforts to catch a thief and claims a mime artist mysteriously appeared in her bed - but by the time the Sloans arrive he has gone. Ken Kercheval guest stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A motocross rider is killed while making a film at a local track, and Dr Sloan suspects sabotage may account for the air of tension hanging over the set

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble becomes a cutman for a fighter who knows he has brain damage but refuses to quit

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry defends a nurse accused of murdering a gossip columnist in a case made more complicated when she confesses to a crime he believes she did not commit

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part two of two. In the guise of a peasant, Walker continues his mission to smash a ring of slave traders

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The body of a university student is found in a burnt-out car, triggering an investigation that leads the team to suspect a jealous boyfriend and a professional debt collector. However, the revelation that the victim died prior to the fire raises questions about the true nature of the crime. Meanwhile, Delko is helped by a mysterious stranger who may be supposedly deceased colleague Tim Speedle

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the disappearance of photographer Martin Roe, who was due to testify at an army first lieutenant's court-martial after he captured a picture of what appeared to be a soldier killing an unarmed civilian. However, the photograph is leaked, and Roe is later found dead with a needle in his arm

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when two pharmaceutical executives are murdered, uncovering a criminal who changes his identity for each offence he commits, and has evaded capture across several countries. To make matters worse, the agents are made to feel the pressure by the watchful eye of Granger. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and Miguel Ferrer

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble finds himself distrusted when he joins a group of fruit harvesters, and must win them over

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Mason accepts a five-dollar retaining fee from a little old lady convinced her employer is about to be murdered - a crime she is eventually charged with

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. Parsons continues his inquiries into Gibbs and his agents, but events take a turn when an explosion kills the Secretary of the US Navy and seriously injures the director of Homeland Security. When attempts are made on the lives of Gibbs and Tony, it appears that all of the NCIS agents are in mortal danger. Starring Mark Harmon and Colin Hanks

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman battles a formidable arms dealer in a fight to the death

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the deaths of a radio DJ and a naval officer who were both killed during a live broadcast, only to discover there are a number of potential suspects. Evidence leads the agents to suspect the involvement of a radical group called Military at Home, prompting Tony to investigate an upmarket gated community - where his door-to-door inquiries yield an enticing proposition or two from the female residents. American drama, starring Michael Weatherly, with Nadia Bjorlin

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. Kensi is taken into custody as chief suspect in the murder of Marine Sergeant Chris Black, but fancies her chances of catching the real killer herself. Meanwhile, Callen and the rest of the team work to prove their colleague's innocence. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah and Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the new Secretary of the Navy, charges Gibbs and the team with finding terrorist group the Brotherhood of Doubt, which was responsible for killing her predecessor. She expects the group's next target to be billionaire Tomas Mendez, who spends his money trying to defeat terrorism, but whose motives may not be entirely altruistic. Meanwhile, Tony flies to Tel Aviv, where new Mossad director Orli Elbaz reveals they have a report of a possible sighting of Ziva at a kibbutz she went to as a child. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A retired American football star is murdered, but his widow jeopardises Alexx's investigation by seizing the body, determined to have it cryonically frozen before an autopsy can be performed. Robbery initially appears to be the motive for the crime - but as events unfold, another possible reason emerges. Meanwhile, Stetler pressures Calleigh and Jake to end their relationship

programme.desc: Abby becomes personally involved in the case of a murdered naval scientist who she regards as a kindred spirit. The team's initial inquiries turn up little concrete information about the victim's life, but the forensic specialist makes a breakthrough when she decodes some of the mysterious markings covering the body. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: Part two of two. Kensi goes in search of her father's killer, before bumping into her mother, who she has not seen for more than 15 years. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah and Miguel Ferrer

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An explosion at an apartment building brings navy officer Terence Keith to the team's attention and it transpires he had been on medication for anxiety, a condition that had led to him being ejected from a pilot's training course. Ducky's profile of Keith suggests that he may be unstable and about to exact revenge for having his dream of becoming a fighter ace taken away from him, and evidence points to him intending to carry out a suicide attack

programme.title: CSI: Miami

programme.desc: It's a testing time for philandering doctor Patrick as he struggles to cope with his grief for Rachel - but his problems are only compounded when her vengeful husband comes looking for him. programme.desc: Arthur must fix a domestic catastrophe and makes up for lost sleep at his birthday celebration. Ricky wonders at David's interest in Bianca, while Phil makes an unwelcome discovery

programme.desc: Will upsets Erica by advertising for a new partner without consulting her. Unfortunately, he only has a short time to mull the matter over before he clashes with Andrew about the welfare of a 12-year-old boy trapped in a bitter custody battle _ and as if that wasn't enough to contend with, practice nurse Laura is run off the road by a fellow driver. Drama, starring Simon Shepherd, Gary Mavers and Saskia Wickham

programme.desc: Part one of two. Paul's first family holiday in six years turns out to be more business than pleasure when a suspected suicide arouses his suspicions. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Alcohol threatens to prevent Yorkshire's first trainee astronaut from doing his job, so Foggy prepares to step into his boots. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A prostitute turns up at the station claiming to have been raped, and as Gently looks back at past cases, he is shocked to discover that very few have been successfully prosecuted - if they made it to court in the first place. Risking animosity from his colleagues, he becomes suspicious when he notices the same police inspector's name appearing repeatedly in the files. To complicate matters, the body of another rape victim is found, prompting a visit to the local brothel. Meanwhile, Bacchus has started an affair with Gemma, the wife of a fellow copper. Martin Shaw, Lee Ingleby and Lisa McGrillis star, with guest appearances from Denise Welch, Derek Riddell and Emily Woof

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A dog is found killed in a manner that matches a series of animal slayings from 30 years earlier. The team reopens the case, but the cruelty of the crimes affects Brian's judgement - especially when his own pet dies. Jack's deteriorating health causes concern for his colleagues and a visit to a museum leads to an alarming discovery. Richard Briers guest stars

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A preserved female body is found buried in a Sheffield peat moor, and DI Sonia Hardwick calls in archaeologist Patrick Cain to help with the investigation, assisted by Nikki. When an examination of the corpse suggests a sexually motivated attack, Leo suspects there is a link to a serial killer case he dealt with in 1985, but following the lead means returning to his home town and confronting the memories of his dead wife and child. Guest starring Mark Benton and Barbara Flynn, with William Gaminara

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: Elizabeth struggles through her labour, while King Henry's envoys embark on a diplomatic mission to Burgundy, a York stronghold abroad. Negotiations are jeopardised by a twist of fate, and Lizzie learns she has more in common with her husband than she first imagined. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy and Michelle Fairley star

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: The York princesses are strategically married off to strengthen the Tudor cause, but further political challenges are on the horizon. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star

programme.desc: A woman's body is found on the moors, and although it looks like suicide, Gently is convinced it is suspicious - even after finding out the victim had mood swings and suffered from motor neurone disease. She was a GP, estranged from her husband, and it isn't long before he is in the frame, although as the investigation moves to the doctor's home community - the same place where Rachel grew up - a secretive local company provides a new lead. programme.desc: Rene, Hans and the Colonel are taken to the chateau's dungeons for interrogation at the hands of Herr Flick and his fiendish organ music. Meanwhile, the British airmen prepare to make their escape by balloon. Wartime comedy, starring Gorden Kaye, Richard Gibson and Richard Marner

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy makes a shocking discovery when he learns he may be a member of the aristocracy. Meanwhile, Compo concludes that wearing a hat is his ticket to winning Nora Batty's heart. Brian Wilde and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: The detective is called out when a body is found on a rubbish pile under the Tyne Bridge. It initially looks like suicide, but evidence reveals the victim was dead before he landed. The search begins to uncover his identity, with just a pawnbroker's receipt, a note with a phone number for one Gwendolyn Howard and a betting slip in the deceased's pocket. Crime drama, guest starring Jim Moir (aka Vic Reeves)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the 10-year-old murder of a librarian who also belonged to a witches' coven, and tries to track down the other members of the group. Halford has visions of his dead wife, and becomes convinced he has been cursed, while Lane starts to suspect the killing may have been part of a human sacrifice. With guest star Stephanie Beacham

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Harry is called to Budapest by Hungarian human rights lawyer Anna Sandor to perform a second post-mortem on the body of a drowned prostitute. He begins to help his lover investigate the suspicious death, but finds himself implicated in her murder when she is killed in her bed. The pathologist calls on Leo for help as he is forced to retreat underground while continuing to work on the case

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: A pretender to the throne is held captive at court, confounding the Tudors, and creating a new rift in Lizzie and King Henry's marriage. Lizzie faces an impossible choice, and makes a risky move to try to eliminate the threat to her family. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: The pretender exposes King Henry's weaknesses, while Lizzie confronts new demons and old curses. Concluding the sequel to The White Queen, set in the Middle Ages as the War of the Roses comes to an end. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades revisits three fixes from the barn - a polyphon, gramophone and transistor radio - to tell the story of how modern music has been influenced by vintage music players

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: In an episode originally broadcast on 27 October 1998, Pat and Peggy clash spectacularly following an accusation that Frank and Pat are having an affair behind their partners' backs. Frank searches for a missing Roy

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian returns to Darwin Ward, determined to make amends with Jac. But when forgiveness does not come easy, Kian goes into self-destruct mode - with almost fatal consequences. Meanwhile, a broken Essie searches for her purpose, but where will she find the answer she seeks? Ric suspects Guy has fallen off the wagon and sets out to stop him working at Holby, once and for all

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: With no money and Rory's health faltering, Agatha finds refuge in the storeroom of her old workplace. Meghan and Jack are at breaking point until Agatha takes a phone call from Hayden, who is in police custody. Laura Carmichael stars

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: In the final episode, the play's producer decides to draft in a voice of experience to get Michael and David in line. Meanwhile, Georgia shares David's work without him knowing, and he is pleasantly surprised by the enthusiastic response his work receives, and Michael seeks resolution on what has happened to his neighbour. Comedy, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Michelle Visage welcomes comedian Keith Lemon, who chats about the appeal of crafting in a pandemic. Former footballer Alex Scott rings to inspire Michelle with her ambitious workouts, and the presenter gets on a treadmill with bestie Leah Remini

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is proud of her working-class roots, but with the gap between rich and poor widening, she wants to know how easy it would be for her to move up a class. She overhauls her leisure activities with a spot of golf and some classical music, takes voice lessons to refine her accent and supercharges her table manners with an intense etiquette lesson. However, she soon discovers that there are obstacles in the way that can make social mobility very difficult

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary following the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The control room experiences a spike in calls for patients with breathing difficulties, and when Sherilee talks to a daughter calls whose mother has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, it proves tough for the call handler as her own mum had the same illness. Meanwhile, call handler Brogan has to guide a caller through lifesaving CPR while they wait for an ambulance, and there's a full emergency at John Lennon Airport when a plane loses contact with air traffic control

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford finds out if drinking cups of tea can increase the risk of cancer, and Danny Crates skips breakfast for a week to see if it helps with weight loss

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a semi-detached house in Romford, Essex, a four-bedroom terrace in Liverpool and a former bank in Nuneaton in Warwickshire

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Footage captures the moment a van teeters on the edge of a motorway bridge after skidding on black ice, and the adventurous pensioner who got lost on Ben Nevis during a snow storm

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and David Harper guide two teams scouring Nottingham racecourse for bargains to take to auction. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Fresh starts are considered in some households, while Leonie's legal skills find an outlet. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Dorset to help a couple with £475,000 find a rural property that fits the bill when it comes to a workshop and providing space to keep hens. Jules also delves in the county's glove-making heritage and produces his own bespoke pair

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Artists create pieces for buyers in Chesterfield who want something special to remember their beloved fathers and stepdad, who they tragically lost in the space of two years

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades traces heroic stories from the Second World War and how they still resonate today, revisiting three fixes from the barn; a watch, swagger stick and belt

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Wallasey to turn Sue's concrete space into a garden fitting for the two rose bushes she planted for her late husband and mother

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jonathan Owen from Sussex arrives at the barn, bearing a small item with a big history for the attention of woodwork specialist Will Kirk. The prized possession is a hand-made model Spitfire plane, carved by Jonathan's Uncle Reggie between bombing raids over Germany. Master saddlemaker Suzie Fletcher tackles the repair of a worn out and badly torn leather pouffe with precious memories for its owner Lou, silversmith Brenton West takes on the restoration of a battered silver purse and mechanical guru Steve Fletcher is faced with how to carry out the delicate repair of an old Victorian rain gauge

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The last group of five well-known faces, tennis coach Judy Murray, broadcaster and internet personality Riyadh Khalaf, Olympic gold medallist and sports presenter Sir Matthew Pinsent CBE, TV personality Pete Wicks, and actor and comedian Crissy Rock, enter the MasterChef kitchen to take on a series of culinary challenges. Crissy, Sir Matthew and Pete head to Sam's Riverside, while Judy and Riyadh go to Scully to cook for paying customers in a busy lunch service, before the contestants head back to the MasterChef kitchen to impress judges John and Gregg with their own creations

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker introduces highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester United v West Ham United, at Old Trafford, Liverpool v Chelsea at Anfield and Aston Villa v Arsenal at Villa Park

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Welsh international footballer Hal Robson-Kanu, British athletics great Colin Jackson, rugby league legend Paul Sculthorpe and world champion boxer Terri Harper join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford finds out if a daily glass of wine is a good thing and Chris Bavin asks if calorie counting is a sensible approach to weight loss

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a terraced house in Wigston, Leicestershire, a flat in Swanscombe, Kent, and a piece of land in Bolton, Lancashire

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Near misses caught on CCTV, including a woman trapped in her loft conversion while a fire rages below her and the race to rescue a boy who has fallen off a promenade into the sea

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Weeks provide expert advice to two teams as the competition heads to the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge. Presenter Anita Manning shows of a Bargain Hunt favourite with a Cornish flavour

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Impending changes come hard for Ryan, Renee, Hazel and Shannon. A letter for Kam sparks excitement

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple look for a home in Derbyshire on a budget of £450,000, and also visits the Royal Crown Derby factory to find out about the porcelain industry

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A stone carver, a fine art painter and a heraldic artist pitch ideas for a piece of art to celebrate the 850th anniversary of a Scottish castle. Hosted by Nick Knowles

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: The repairs of a cash register, a camera and a calculator, three pre-digital items that helped shape the modern computerised world people take for granted today

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley investigate counterfeit buffalo mozzarella being sold on supermarket pizzas, and potentially dangerous hairdryers. They also tell the story of a website racket that robbed people of millions of pounds, and Michelle finds out what's real and what's fake in the world of celebrity memorabilia

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining four celebrities face two more challenges, beginning with the pairs task. Here they are split into teams and have to each serve up either John Torode's sushi selection or his veal schnitzel with spaetzle - all while separated by a wall. The second challenge sees them entering the world of mass catering at Leyton Orient Football Club

programme.title: Dear NHS Superstars
programme.desc: A selection of personal stories from some of Britain's best-loved stars paying tribute to the work of the NHS. Amanda Holden, Lenny Henry, Jo Brand and Alex Brooker are among the famous faces sharing their admiration for healthcare staff who have touched their lives in all sorts of ways. Others expressing their gratitude include Jack Whitehall, Adrian Lester, Shirley Ballas, Phil Wang, Rosie Jones, Kevin Bridges, Konnie Huq, Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc. Presented by writer, comedian and former doctor Adam Kay

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob talks to the poet and playwright, who recently published his memoir My Name Is Why. He discusses what it was like growing up as the only black child in a sleepy market town outside Wigan in the 1970s, his time in care and how he was separated from his foster family at the age of 12 and left to fend for himself. He also returns to Ethiopia to find out more about his roots. Featuring contributions from colleagues including Steve Coogan, Benjamin Zephaniah, Linton Kwesi Johnson and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: UCOS looks into the murder of a private investigator who was stabbed through the heart in a suspected robbery gone wrong. The answers may lie with the victim's last three cases, but it'll take the whole team's expertise to crack the codes within his mysterious notebook. The movements of a rare stamp could hold the key to the investigation - if only UCOS could track it down. Meanwhile, Steve has problems when he rents out his flat

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A fibre artist, a ceramicist and an abstract painter create artworks for a Staffordshire who woman wants a piece to mark the birth of her first grandchild. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: With the help of archive, Jay Blades charts how three inventions fixed in the barn - a magic lantern, projector and mutoscope - helped influence modern TV and film technology

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: From her garden at Glebe Cottage in Devon, Carol Klein goes back to the basics of propagating plants by demonstrating how to take cuttings. There is also an interview with Advolly Richmond, whose love of garden history is reflected in her own garden, and she shares her passion for a flower with a surprising past. Joe Swift has a large plant in his London garden that is due for some timely remedial work, and the team travels to Brighton to visit a garden packed with exotic plants grown in raised beds and containers

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The series concludes with a visit to the country's eastern seaboard to investigate how the Chinese government is managing environmental concerns while meeting the ever-expanding needs of the population. Among the region's diverse locales lie futuristic cities, seaweed-thatched villages and rugged wetlands that are home to some of the world's rarest animal species

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nick Crane explores the wealth of wildlife attracted to the Firth of Forth. Tessa Dunlop reveals how the Victorian zeal for cleanliness turned the Thames into a giant toilet bowl

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: The animal families face the formidable monsoon rains and the giant otter cubs must watch out for predatory jaguars and learn to swim before their river floods. The coatis have to eat as much as they can ahead of their habitat disappearing underwater, while the youngest capuchin monkey needs to master some clever survival skills. Stephen Mangan narrates

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: A look back at days seven and eight of the Rio Olympics, where British focus was on the likes of Mo Farah, Bradley Wiggins, Helen Glover and Laura Kenny

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ushaw in Co Durham, antique experts David Fletcher and James Lewis put a value on the region's antiques and collectibles. David is delighted by a 1950s toy plane, while James finds a 1940s menu from a famous Hollywood restaurant which has been signed by some top film stars. Host Paul Martin visits the Mining Art Gallery in Bishop Auckland to find out why many coal miners were inspired to pick up a paintbrush

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Jeremy Vine in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the south east of Australia from Canberra, the seat of government, to the multi-ethnic heart of its second largest city, Melbourne. Floating above Canberra in a hot-air balloon, Michael sees how Australia's 'Bush Capital' was planned in the early 20th century and tours the nation's Parliament. He takes the high-speed train nearly 300 miles south west, to arrive in Melbourne at the head of Port Philip Bay on Australia's south-eastern coast

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Ellises sample life in the 1970s, an age of power cuts and strikes, but also a golden era for working class families. They enjoy rare time together helped with the advent of their first record player and car, while actress Claire Sweeney brings 'Scouse', her family's speciality, round for tea. Sara Cox and Polly Russell present

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti join the staff working in some of the most unusual hotels around the world, learning the tricks of the trade along the way. This time, Giles and Monica help out at The Torridon in the Scottish Highlands, Britain's most northerly five-star hotel. Its need to balance a luxury stay with being self-sufficient has Monica collecting seaweed for the kitchen and helping to herd Highland cattle, while Giles learns to change toilet seats and joins the staff in their afternoon off - for a swim in the beautiful but freezing-cold loch

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: A huge scandal threatens Rupert's dream of passing on the family business to one of his children, resulting in 'the most humble day' of his life. In response to the revelations, the Murdoch Empire goes on the offensive and the alliance against him come under sustained pressure, but whistle-blowers begin to emerge, then a bombshell revelation completely changes public opinion. A sequence of disastrous events leaves Murdoch's business plans in ruins, and he closes the News of the World newspaper

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Jon Richardson, team captain on 8 Out of 10 Cats, hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, west London, warming up the crowd before introducing routines by fellow comedians Sara Pascoe and Nathan Caton

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan: From My Sofa
programme.desc: Romesh catches up with old friends from Albania, Mongolia and The Arctic, revisiting some of the highlights and introducing never-before-seen moments such as his near-death experience in a high-speed Mongolia car rally

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades narrates a spin-off show delving deeper into three repairs to show how the heirlooms help shaped modern life, beginning with the story of toys

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson keeps an eye on his cuttings and Kirsty Wilson is extra careful as she shows how to propagate cacti. There is good news and bad news on an allotment in Aberdeen

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants are split into pairs and tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour - but there's a catch. They are separated by a wall, meaning the key to the task is communication as one pair recreates John Torode's pan-fried cod wrapped in ham and the other tackles his calf's liver and champ mash. For the second task, the competitors are sent out on their first mass catering challenge, at the McLaren Technology Centre in Woking, Surrey

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges an impressionist painter, a metal sculptor and an artist who paints in a caravan to create artwork for a London couple who have just renovated their home

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades revisits three fixes from the barn - a polyphon, gramophone and transistor radio - to tell the story of how modern music has been influenced by vintage music players

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits the Thai capital of Bangkok, where he learns how the late 19th-century King Rama V built a grand palace and introduced railways to the country. Michael also learns that King Rama V's love of steam engines was inspired by Queen Victoria. Moving on, the presenter also confronts his fear of snakes to assist in a surgical operation on a venomous king cobra and steps into the ring to learn Muay Thai boxing

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the survival techniques used by the animal kingdom's smallest creatures. North America's Sonoran Desert is home to the grasshopper mouse, which is capable of disarming scorpions and centipedes twice its size, and displays a power and athleticism that enables it to avoid the attentions of rattlesnakes and tarantulas. In Africa, the elephant shrew, known as the sengi, employs speed to keep its distance from predators, following a meticulously constructed - and maintained - network of tiny racetracks in the savannah. The film concludes with Hidden Kingdoms Revealed, a 10-minute report on how the documentary was made. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len Goodman and a celebrity guest reminisce about one of their favourite holidays. This time, BBC weather presenter Carol Kirkwood revisits St Helier in Jersey

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team travels to the Netherlands, where Nick Crane explores how Dutch engineers created massive coastal defences following the North Sea floods in 1953 that killed thousands of people. Historian Tessa Dunlop investigates why the tulip trade nearly bankrupted the country 400 years ago, Mark Horton discovers how a large area of coastal land was drained to create new living space, and Miranda Krestovnikoff finds out about raw herring becoming a popular seaside snack. Meanwhile, Countryfile's Adam Henson traces the history of the Friesian cow trade with Britain

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: A fierce drought culminates in huge and ferocious fires, and the capuchin monkeys, giant otters and coatis must use their survival skills to endure the extreme conditions. The animals are also looking for mates so they can breed in time to ensure the next generation are born at the optimal moment

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: Days nine and 10 of the Rio Olympics, which saw gymnast Max Whitlock lead the way by claiming two gold medals in one day with victory in the pommel horse and floor exercises

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and David Fletcher scour a crowd at Ushaw in Co Durham to find objects to take off to auction. Catherine uncovers a large collection of glassware and stoneware dug up in the north east of England, while David finds an American money box in the shape of a bulldog. Host Paul Martin visits the Oriental Museum at Durham University to find out about the collection of Malcolm McDonald, a British diplomat who was passionate about Asian ceramics

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Quiz show, presented by Jeremy Vine in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his exploration of southeast Australia in Melbourne, Victoria, where he visits a home in the suburbs of Lilydale that once belonged to Dame Nellie Melba. Later, he travels through the rainforest of the Dandenong mountains across a spectacular trestle bridge, before reaching St Kilda, where he enjoys a special screening of a feature film that explores the exploits of the notorious outlaw, Ned Kelly

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Ellises sample life in the 1980s and 1990s, and experience their first telephone, chest freezer and toastie maker. The girls also absorb the Manchester music scene with Shaun Ryder, before the family have a millennium party. Sara Cox and Polly Russell present

programme.title: Bears About the House
programme.desc: Part two of two. Conservationist Giles Clark continues his biggest mission to date - taking on the illegal wildlife trade and helping to build a new bear sanctuary in Laos, south-east Asia. At the sanctuary, Mary the sun bear is now nine months old. Now growing in confidence, she is becoming extremely determined, and picks up a skill unique to a sun bear, jumping. Later, the sanctuary receives some shocking news when they discover there has been a break-in, and the distraught team have to come to terms with a theft

programme.title: Mrs America
programme.desc: It's 1973, and with 33 of the necessary 38 states ratifying the Equal Rights Amendment, Phyllis Schlafly's campaign has polarised opinion in the critical state of Illinois. The proponents offer her a challenge to publicly debate the issues, but are surprised by a counter-offer for a 'couple's debate' on national television. Drama about the political movement to pass the Equal Rights Amendment in the US. Starring Cate Blanchett, Rose Byrne, Uzo Aduba and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Mrs America
programme.desc: In February 1975, Jill Ruckelshaus is appointed chairwoman of a National Commission to honour women and urge ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment, but she has to contend with Phyllis Schlafly. Starring Cate Blanchett, Rose Byrne, Uzo Aduba and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels to the Shetland Isles, meeting Brydon Thomason from Shetland Nature on the island of Yell

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: A look at the impact of the insurgency from the perspectives of American soldier Lt Col Nate Sassaman and Baghdadi Alaa Adel, who was 12 when she suffered life-changing injuries

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades narrates a look at three unique items from the home front during Second World War - a doll, a music box, and a homemade telescope built by an air raid warden

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: The final episode of the documentary features a tour of an extraordinary new home - Villa Troglodyte, which has come onto the market. Built as if carved out of the rock, the price is 'only' £23million because it doesn't have a sea view. The pressure to build more houses in such a small country has left Prince Albert with a challenge. Cameras follow him as he attends meetings about a huge two-billion-euro land extension project he has initiated. Extending into the sea is a complex operation, but once complete the largest villas there are estimated to sell for around 100million euros

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the four remaining contestants battling for semi-final places, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - finalist and TV presenter Rylan Clark-Neal and champions Sophie Thompson and Kimberly Wyatt. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders push the boat out in a bid to secure their semi-final place

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Artists create pieces for buyers in Chesterfield who want something special to remember their beloved fathers and a stepdad, who they lost within the space of two years

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades traces heroic stories from the Second World War and how they still resonate today, revisiting three fixes from the barn; a watch, swagger stick and belt

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with workers at the Walkers Crisps factory in Leicester, which he visited in 2016, finding out how they are coping during the coronavirus crisis. He speaks to manager Carl Lewis, who reveals that in order to keep up with demand they're making big changes to their output, switching away from single packets to a situation where 90 per cent of production is now the multipacks sold in supermarkets

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates another documentary revealing the survival techniques of the animal kingdom's smallest creatures. This film follows a young tree shrew as it darts through the rainforest of Borneo on the lookout for food. The creature's super-fast metabolism means it is always hungry, but as it searches for fruit, it must avoid fire ants, giant snakes and even a carnivorous plant. Cameras also follow a male chipmunk collecting acorns in preparation for winter - but while he's out, a bigger, more aggressive neighbour ransacks his store. Can he get the nuts back?

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: The Spanish Main (1945)
programme.desc: Dutch refugees shipwrecked on the coast of Cartagene in Spain are imprisoned by a villainous colonial governor. However, the leader of the group manages to escape and goes on to become a feared pirate captain. He then returns five years later to exact revenge. Swashbuckling adventure, starring Paul Henreid, Maureen O'Hara, Walter Slezak and Binnie Barnes

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes actress Lisa Riley - best known to Emmerdale fans as Mandy Dingle on a journey to Bronte Country - Haworth in West Yorkshire - to re-create a family trip there in 1992. The pair travel in a Citroen CX, just like the one Lisa's mum drove all those years ago, and after the obligatory Barbra Streisand singalong, Lisa explains the importance of the Bronte sisters on her childhood. Along the way, Len and Lisa take a trip on a steam train, visit the Bronte memorial chapel and chat about the actress's career

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: A look back at three more days of the Rio Olympics, which saw Laura Trott and Jason Kenny claim their second and third gold medals of the Games respectively

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Michael Baggott assess items at Barrow Hill Roundhouse in Derbyshire and Paul Martin visits the village of Hathersage, which provided inspiration for Jane Eyre

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Quiz show, presented by Jeremy Vine in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by rail through Queensland from Kuranda to Townsville, marvelling at the engineering expertise of a railway line built in the 1890s. He also visits a plantation where he learns how to harvest sugar cane and ventures into the hinterland, where he braves a crocodile safari

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Ellis family reflects on their time-travelling adventure, and explores how the legacy of 100 years of history lives on in the northern diet today. Lesley and Sara Cox visit a Liverpool bakery, and sisters Caitlin and Freya examine how recent changes on Manchester's Curry Mile affect peoples' evolving relationship with flavour. Finally Sara and northern chef Rob Owen Brown prepare a celebration meal for the family

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The contestants do the make-up for London Fashion Week models, replicating the look demonstrated by Dominic Skinner - and with tight deadlines to meet, the pressure is on. As if that were not bad enough, fashion designer Henry Holland is the guest judge, helping to decide which two hopefuls will receive the 15-minute penalty in the next challenge, which involves creating a look based on a personal icon

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of 'radio activity', with Joe Lycett, Shazia Mirza, Josh Widdicombe and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: Emily is getting excited about her favourite night of the year, Simon's annual pub quiz. However, when the evening arrives, Gandalf has a fall and cannot babysit, leaving her with a choice - miss the quiz or take Rosie along to the pub. In the early timeline, Emily goes back to work and puts Rosie into a nursery, only for the youngster to suffer a frightening medical emergency

programme.title: Frankie Boyle Live: Excited for You to See and Hate This
programme.desc: The comedian takes to the stage of the King's Theatre in his home town of Glasgow after completing his first major tour of Scotland in 12 years. Frankie turns his attention to a number of diverse subjects, including Glasgow's relationship with swearing and vitamins, British politics, his concerns about ageing, buying a dog and the nature of comedy itself

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: A huge scandal threatens Rupert's dream of passing on the family business to one of his children, resulting in 'the most humble day' of his life. In response to the revelations, the Murdoch Empire goes on the offensive and the alliance against him come under sustained pressure, but whistle-blowers begin to emerge, then a bombshell revelation completely changes public opinion. A sequence of disastrous events leaves Murdoch's business plans in ruins, and he closes the News of the World newspaper

programme.title: Alex Brooker: Disability and Me
programme.desc: The Last Leg presenter confronts what disability really means in Britain today. Alex is reunited with a childhood friend who has spina bifida, who challenges him to consider the impact of making jokes about his own disability. He also pays a visit to a bespoke disability helpline, meets members of Arsenal's disabled supporters club, and talks to Paralympic swimmer Susie Rodgers about her life

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: The presenter reports on how southern Spain has become the main gateway for drugs into Europe, leading to violent turf wars between gangs and a government crackdown as the police struggle to regain control of their streets. Stacey goes on aerial patrol with police officers guarding the strip of water between Morocco and Spain, and discovers the extraordinary lengths smugglers will take to evade them, including travelling at 100mph in speedboats during extreme weather conditions

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Traversing the rugged and mysterious north Cornish coast, explorer Paul Rose's first stop is Morwenstow, where he squeezes into the Trust's smallest property, the Hawker's Hut. Built in the 1800s by opium-smoking poet Reverend Hawker, it is popular with walkers who add their own graffiti. Next, Paul enjoys a tour of the wild and majestic Tintagel Castle, before heading to Padstow where the Mayday 'Obby 'Oss celebrations are in full swing. Paul is then put through his paces by a group of youngsters training to be surf lifesavers at Newquay's Fistral beach

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: As night settles over a rocky coast, Bob Ross produces a warm glow in the sky in a painting on black canvas

programme.title: Bob Ross: The Happy Painter
programme.desc: Documentary taking a behind-the-scenes look at American painter Bob Ross's journey from humble beginnings to pop culture icon, charting how one local TV commercial evolved into a viral phenomenon that continues to inspire millions around the world. The film reveals the public and private sides of Bob Ross through accounts from close friends and family, childhood photographs and rare archive footage. Interviewees recount his gentle, mild-mannered demeanour and unwavering dedication to wildlife as well as disclosing little-known facts about his famous curly hair - which was naturally straight - and his fascination with fast cars

programme.title: Empire of the Tsars: Romanov Russia with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley tells the story of the dynasty that ruled Russia for more than three centuries, including figures such as Peter the Great and Catherine the Great, the struggle against Napoleon in 1812, and the political murders of Nicholas II and his family in 1918. In the first edition, she investigates the beginning of the Romanovs' 300-year reign in Russia, and charts ruthless Tsar Peter the Great's rule, who acceded to the throne at the age of nine

programme.title: Russia on Four Wheels
programme.desc: Part one of two. Justin Rowlatt and Anita Rani set off on separate road trips across Russia from Sochi, the Black Sea resort hosting the 2014 Winter Olympics. Anita travels north and west in a Kombat, a Russian-built luxury armour-plated vehicle, and enters a thriving world of westernised entrepreneurs who are at the forefront of shaping a new and vibrant country. Meanwhile, Justin heads east in a Soviet-built UAZ jeep, encountering a world that seems hardly to have changed since the Cold War

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of the world's most outstanding rhythmic gymnast Margarita Mamun, the leading representative of the Russian national team. The film presents a groundbreaking year in her life, from the 2015 World Championships to the 2016 Olympic Games, offering unprecedented access to the hidden world of elite gymnasts and the challenging training regime demanded by the Russian system

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank: At Home with the British
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank explores Britain's love affair with the terrace, charting the highs of Victorian industry, the lows of their post-war decline and on to their 21st-century regeneration - which for one street in Toxteth, Liverpool, even saw them awarded the 2015 Turner Prize

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Multimedia artist Rachel Maclean's film exploring societal pressures on women from the media. Two inmates in a surreal prison are forced to compete against each other in performing a series of strange tasks, but they find a way to subvert the rules of the system and expose the sinister truth that underpins their world

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: As night settles over a rocky coast, Bob Ross produces a warm glow in the sky in a painting on black canvas

programme.title: Empire of the Tsars: Romanov Russia with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley tells the story of the dynasty that ruled Russia for more than three centuries, including figures such as Peter the Great and Catherine the Great, the struggle against Napoleon in 1812, and the political murders of Nicholas II and his family in 1918. In the first edition, she investigates the beginning of the Romanovs' 300-year reign in Russia, and charts ruthless Tsar Peter the Great's rule, who acceded to the throne at the age of nine

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Paul Rose explores the spectacular coastline of southern Cornwall, discovering one of the world's finest stages at the Minack open-air theatre in Porthcurno. He also tries out the high-octane sport of coasteering on the Lizard Peninsula, joins a National Trust ranger for some clifftop wildlife spotting, and fires the Tudor cannons of Pendennis Castle in Falmouth

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paints a wintry mountain scene using only grey and white tones

programme.title: The Human Body: Secrets of Your Life Revealed
programme.desc: Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore the secrets of the body, beginning with the changes it goes through. They examine why human childhood is longer than any other creature on earth, reveal the communities of microbes - the microbiome - that people cultivate throughout their lives, and uncover the mysterious trigger from child to adult

programme.title: Wild Swimming with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: Alice Roberts embarks on a personal quest to discover what lies behind the new-found passion for swimming in the wild that is sweeping Britain, visiting rivers and underground lakes and taking a skinny dip in a moorland pool

programme.title: Swimming Through the Seasons - The Hampstead Ponds
programme.desc: Documentary filmed over 12 months following people who use three swimming ponds on Hampstead Heath, where they meet to take on the weather, the water and life in general

programme.title: Being Beethoven
programme.desc: As the composer embarked on the extraordinary flowering of his late music, his health deteriorated and his desire for love and family tipped over into obsession

programme.title: Bob Ross: The Happy Painter
programme.desc: Documentary that takes a behind-the-scenes look at American painter Bob Ross's journey from humble beginnings to pop culture icon

programme.title: Andrew Marr's Great Scots: The Writers Who Shaped a Nation
programme.desc: The life of Walter Scott, the prolific novelist and poet who wrote swashbuckling tales of romance and derring-do. However, Andrew Marr reveals his lesser-known side as a political fixer who believed in a proud Scotland inside the UK. Scott brought George IV to his home country and, swathing him in tartan, created an enduring myth of Scotland as a place of romance populated by a brave race doing brave deeds, all clad in kilts

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paints a wintry mountain scene using only grey and white tones

programme.title: Wild Swimming with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: Alice Roberts embarks on a personal quest to discover what lies behind the new-found passion for swimming in the wild that is sweeping Britain, visiting rivers and underground lakes and taking a skinny dip in a moorland pool

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Paul Rose continues his journey by exploring the soft and rolling coastline of south Devon, taking a snorkelling trip around Burgh Island and hitching a ride on the Dartmouth to Paignton steam train

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a lakeside view, surrounded by golden autumn trees

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: While continuing her efforts to set up Harriet and the vicar, Emma looks forward to meeting Frank Churchill at the Christmas party, but when he fails to show up she becomes the subject of Mr Elton's unwanted attention. Later, village gossip focuses on the arrival of Jane Fairfax and a piano she was given by a mystery admirer, leaving the matchmaker reluctant to believe Mr Knightley could have sent the gift. Drama, starring Romola Garai, Louise Dylan, Blake Ritson and Laura Pyper

programme.title: Rome Unpacked
programme.desc: Chef Giorgio Locatelli and art critic Andrew Graham-Dixon explore Italy's capital in search of the generations of ordinary Romans who have left their mark on the city's culture and gastronomy. In the first of two programmes, Giorgio leads Andrew to some of his personal favourite districts including Garbatella, Italy's first 'garden suburb', which boasts vibrant market stalls and village architecture, where he introduces him to the simplest Roman food. In turn, Andrew introduces Giorgio to some of the most moving pictures by Caravaggio, in what was once the city's foremost church for poor pilgrims

programme.title: Rome Unpacked
programme.desc: Chef Giorgio Locatelli and art critic Andrew Graham-Dixon conclude their exploration of Italy's capital. In the second of two programmes, as well as marvelling at the mosaics in the 12th-century Basilica di San Clemente, Andrew takes Giorgio to an ancient Roman schoolteacher's classroom. Then it's on to a true architectural and civic wonder - the vast Testaccio Slaughterhouse, where workers were once paid in offal. Travelling to the Palazzo Colonna, Andrew shows Giorgio the Beaneater by Carracci, a Baroque painting that makes an everyday subject extraordinary

programme.title: The Treasures of Ancient Rome
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke explores the artistic achievements of the Romans, tracing their progression from thieves and copycats to pioneers of the use of unflinching realism. Portraits from the Republic reveal how they captured lifelike images and immortalised figures such as Julius Caesar and Cicero using marble, bronze and paint. Alastair also looks at how Rome's first emperor, Augustus, used art as a tool to forge his dominion

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon tells the story of the Royal Collection's remarkable resurrection. Following its fortunes from Charles II through to the 18th century and the enlightened collecting of George III, cameras reveal some of its greatest treasures, including a jewel-encrusted salt cellar in the shape of a castle and a gold State Coach

programme.title: Bought with Love: The Secret History of British Art Collections
programme.desc: Art historian Helen Rosslyn continues to explore impressive country houses, focusing on the Grand Tour era of the 18th century when aristocrats filled their homes with masterpieces by 17th-century classical painters. She visits Holkham Hall in Norfolk to view the collection there, explores the legacy of the Dukes of Richmond at Goodwood House, and learns how the one-time Lord Egremont of Petworth House in Sussex patronised JMW Turner

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a lakeside view, surrounded by golden autumn trees

programme.title: Rome Unpacked

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary following the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The control room experiences a spike in calls for patients with breathing difficulties, and when Sherilee talks to a daughter calls whose mother has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, it proves tough for the call handler as her own mum had the same illness. Meanwhile, call handler Brogan has to guide a caller through lifesaving CPR while they wait for an ambulance, and there's a full emergency at John Lennon Airport when a plane loses contact with air traffic control

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: The rugby legend and wife Maureen travel along the Monmouthshire and Brecon canal, sampling the breath-taking views of the Brecon Beacons. Determined to show that he has mastered the art of narrow-boating, Gareth takes charge of the Bentley of Barges. As a reward for their endeavours, the pair discover how to make gin with ingredients found in the hedgerow. When the canal passes the Glanusk Estate, Gareth seizes the chance to enjoy a spot of fly fishing. Later, in Brecon, they enjoy the performance of a jazz band - a style of music with which the town is synonymous thanks to its annual festival

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jonathan Owen from Sussex arrives at the barn, bearing a small item with a big history for the attention of woodwork specialist Will Kirk. The prized possession is a hand-made model Spitfire plane, carved by Jonathan's Uncle Reggie between bombing raids over Germany. Master saddlemaker Suzie Fletcher tackles the repair of a worn out and badly torn leather pouffe with precious memories for its owner Lou, silversmith Brenton West takes on the restoration of a battered silver purse and mechanical guru Steve Fletcher is faced with how to carry out the delicate repair of an old Victorian rain gauge

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The last group of five well-known faces, tennis coach Judy Murray, broadcaster and internet personality Riyadh Khalaf, Olympic gold medallist and sports presenter Sir Matthew Pinsent CBE, TV personality Pete Wicks, and actor and comedian Crissy Rock, enter the MasterChef kitchen to take on a series of culinary challenges. Crissy, Sir Matthew and Pete head to Sam's Riverside, while Judy and Riyadh go to Scully to cook for paying customers in a busy lunch service, before the contestants head back to the MasterChef kitchen to impress judges John and Gregg with their own creations

programme.desc: Gary Lineker introduces highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester United v West Ham United, at Old Trafford, Liverpool v Chelsea at Anfield and Aston Villa v Arsenal at Villa Park

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Land of the Giants
programme.desc: Sue Perkins narrates a documentary shining a light on the world of people who grow outsize vegetables, following them from March to August as they gear up for the first big competition of the season at the Vale of Glamorgan Agricultural Show

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining four celebrities face two more challenges, beginning with the pairs task. Here they are split into teams and have to each serve up either John Torode's sushi selection or his veal schnitzel with spaetzle - all while separated by a wall. The second challenge sees them entering the world of mass catering at Leyton Orient Football Club

programme.title: Dear NHS Superstars
programme.desc: A selection of personal stories from some of Britain's best-loved stars paying tribute to the work of the NHS. Amanda Holden, Lenny Henry, Jo Brand and Alex Brooker are among the famous faces sharing their admiration for healthcare staff who have touched their lives in all sorts of ways. Others expressing their gratitude include Jack Whitehall, Adrian Lester, Shirley Ballas, Phil Wang, Rosie Jones, Kevin Bridges, Konnie Huq, Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc. Presented by writer, comedian and former doctor Adam Kay

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob talks to the poet and playwright, who recently published his memoir My Name Is Why. He discusses what it was like growing up as the only black child in a sleepy market town outside Wigan in the 1970s, his time in care and how he was separated from his foster family at the age of 12 and left to fend for himself. He also returns to Ethiopia to find out more about his roots. Featuring contributions from colleagues including Steve Coogan, Benjamin Zephaniah, Linton Kwesi Johnson and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: UCOS looks into the murder of a private investigator who was stabbed through the heart in a suspected robbery gone wrong. The answers may lie with the victim's last three cases, but it'll take the whole team's expertise to crack the codes within his mysterious notebook. The movements of a rare stamp could hold the key to the investigation - if only UCOS could track it down. Meanwhile, Steve has problems when he rents out his flat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: In an episode originally broadcast on 27 October 1998, Pat and Peggy clash spectacularly following an accusation that Frank and Pat are having an affair behind their partners' backs. Frank searches for a missing Roy

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian returns to Darwin Ward, determined to make amends with Jac. But when forgiveness does not come easy, Kian goes into self-destruct mode - with almost fatal consequences. Meanwhile, a broken Essie searches for her purpose, but where will she find the answer she seeks? Ric suspects Guy has fallen off the wagon and sets out to stop him working at Holby, once and for all

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: With no money and Rory's health faltering, Agatha finds refuge in the storeroom of her old workplace. Meghan and Jack are at breaking point until Agatha takes a phone call from Hayden, who is in police custody. Laura Carmichael stars

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: In the final episode, the play's producer decides to draft in a voice of experience to get Michael and David in line. Meanwhile, Georgia shares David's work without him knowing, and he is pleasantly surprised by the enthusiastic response his work receives, and Michael seeks resolution on what has happened to his neighbour. Comedy, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is proud of her working-class roots, but with the gap between rich and poor widening, she wants to know how easy it would be for her to move up a class. She overhauls her leisure activities with a spot of golf and some classical music, takes voice lessons to refine her accent and supercharges her table manners with an intense etiquette lesson. However, she soon discovers that there are obstacles in the way that can make social mobility very difficult

programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Wallasey to turn Sue's concrete space into a garden fitting for the two rose bushes she planted for her late husband and mother

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jonathan Owen from Sussex arrives at the barn, bearing a small item with a big history for the attention of woodwork specialist Will Kirk. The prized possession is a hand-made model Spitfire plane, carved by Jonathan's Uncle Reggie between bombing raids over Germany. Master saddlemaker Suzie Fletcher tackles the repair of a worn out and badly torn leather pouffe with precious memories for its owner Lou, silversmith Brenton West takes on the restoration of a battered silver purse and mechanical guru Steve Fletcher is faced with how to carry out the delicate repair of an old Victorian rain gauge

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The last group of five well-known faces, tennis coach Judy Murray, broadcaster and internet personality Riyadh Khalaf, Olympic gold medallist and sports presenter Sir Matthew Pinsent CBE, TV personality Pete Wicks, and actor and comedian Crissy Rock, enter the MasterChef kitchen to take on a series of culinary challenges. Crissy, Sir Matthew and Pete head to Sam's Riverside, while Judy and Riyadh go to Scully to cook for paying customers in a busy lunch service, before the contestants head back to the MasterChef kitchen to impress judges John and Gregg with their own creations

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker introduces highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester United v West Ham United, at Old Trafford, Liverpool v Chelsea at Anfield and Aston Villa v Arsenal at Villa Park

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Welsh international footballer Hal Robson-Kanu, British athletics great Colin Jackson, rugby league legend Paul Sculthorpe and world champion boxer Terri Harper join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford finds out if a daily glass of wine is a good thing and Chris Bavin asks if calorie counting is a sensible approach to weight loss

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a terraced house in Wigston, Leicestershire, a flat in Swanscombe, Kent, and a piece of land in Bolton, Lancashire

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Near misses caught on CCTV, including a woman trapped in her loft conversion while a fire rages below her and the race to rescue a boy who has fallen off a promenade into the sea

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Weeks provide expert advice to two teams as the competition heads to the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge. Presenter Anita Manning shows of a Bargain Hunt favourite with a Cornish flavour

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Impending changes come hard for Ryan, Renee, Hazel and Shannon. A letter for Kam sparks excitement

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple look for a home in Derbyshire on a budget of £450,000, and also visits the Royal Crown Derby factory to find out about the porcelain industry

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A stone carver, a fine art painter and a heraldic artist pitch ideas for a piece of art to celebrate the 850th anniversary of a Scottish castle. Hosted by Nick Knowles

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: The repairs of a cash register, a camera and a calculator, three pre-digital items that helped shape the modern computerised world people take for granted today

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley investigate counterfeit buffalo mozzarella being sold on supermarket pizzas, and potentially dangerous hairdryers. They also tell the story of a website racket that robbed people of millions of pounds, and Michelle finds out what's real and what's fake in the world of celebrity memorabilia

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining four celebrities face two more challenges, beginning with the pairs task. Here they are split into teams and have to each serve up either John Torode's sushi selection or his veal schnitzel with spaetzle - all while separated by a wall. The second challenge sees them entering the world of mass catering at Leyton Orient Football Club

programme.title: Dear NHS Superstars
programme.desc: A selection of personal stories from some of Britain's best-loved stars paying tribute to the work of the NHS. Amanda Holden, Lenny Henry, Jo Brand and Alex Brooker are among the famous faces sharing their admiration for healthcare staff who have touched their lives in all sorts of ways. Others expressing their gratitude include Jack Whitehall, Adrian Lester, Shirley Ballas, Phil Wang, Rosie Jones, Kevin Bridges, Konnie Huq, Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc. Presented by writer, comedian and former doctor Adam Kay

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: The story of a man who abandoned a financial career to pursue a childhood ambition to become a professional rabbit-catcher. Steven McGonigal rejects modern pest control methods such as gas or poisons as environmentally dangerous, instead favouring the traditional skills he learned during his childhood in Magilligan, which involve using ferrets and dogs to herd animals towards him, so he can humanely kill them

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob talks to the poet and playwright, who recently published his memoir My Name Is Why. He discusses what it was like growing up as the only black child in a sleepy market town outside Wigan in the 1970s, his time in care and how he was separated from his foster family at the age of 12 and left to fend for himself. He also returns to Ethiopia to find out more about his roots. Featuring contributions from colleagues including Steve Coogan, Benjamin Zephaniah, Linton Kwesi Johnson and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: UCOS looks into the murder of a private investigator who was stabbed through the heart in a suspected robbery gone wrong. The answers may lie with the victim's last three cases, but it'll take the whole team's expertise to crack the codes within his mysterious notebook. The movements of a rare stamp could hold the key to the investigation - if only UCOS could track it down. Meanwhile, Steve has problems when he rents out his flat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The World Health Organisation urged countries to stamp out infections as soon as they developed, but the UK government's initial scientific advice said the route out of the crisis was for most of us to catch the virus so we could develop 'herd immunity'. Dr Faye Kirkland investigates this approach to the Covid-19 pandemic, asking whether it was a dangerous gamble with people's lives or a sound scientific method. Faced with a growing backlash and warnings that the NHS was close to being overwhelmed, politicians denied herd immunity was the policy and within a few days Britain pivoted to a new strategy - lockdown

programme.desc: Stoick, Hiccup and Snotlout search for Spitelout when he goes missing, and ultimately find him on a distant island battling a fiery Singetail dragon

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Following the activities of a 10-week-old polar bear being raised by his mother in the Canadian Arctic. Percy was born during the winter in a den kept up to 40 degrees warmer than outside, where the temperature drops to minus 60C and it is dark for months. Polar bears have thick coats, a 10cm thick layer of blubber that acts like thermal underwear, and huge fur-covered paws that are like snowshoes

programme.title: O Mo Dhùthaich (From Uist with Love)
programme.desc: In South Uist gamekeeper Rory goes on a deer cull, Archie and Ben prepare their sheep for the North Uist show and Neil's students try their hand at dry-stone walling

programme.desc: Winter sports, including ski jumps, bob sleds and motor skiing

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports

programme.title: Na Tiristich (From Tiree with Love)
programme.desc: The Tiree watersports club is a big hit with youngsters on the island, while Rhoda, Alastair and Iain take their lambs to the lamb sale and Tiree musicians take centre stage

programme.title: All That Lies Behind Me (Sgeulachd Donald Merrett)
programme.desc: Using dramatic reconstructions and expert opinion, John Morrison revisits the story of John Donald Merrett - a murderer who, having been cleared of killing his mother in 1927, spent the next 30 years on a spree across Europe, spending a large inheritance on women and the black market. The programme also reveals how when Merrett returned to Britain, he killed his wife and mother-in-law, before committing suicide

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: The uplifting and inspiring story of beekeeping in Scotland today, offering a closer look at these amazing creatures and the challenges they must overcome to ensure their survival

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann Nic Amhlaoibh cross borders and traditions from the Western Isles to the west coast of Ireland in a quest to find their shared musical heritage. The duo are welcomed by various pipers, singers and musicians en route, including Daoiri Farrell in Dublin

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
