programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team visit Walsall to help members of the public sort the treasures from their trash, with items including a Rolex watch and a 300-year-old pocket timepiece

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five university students from Belfast answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: It is the annual Portwenn versus Port Carran gig race and school gig festival, and this time Penhale is the umpire and Morwenna is the Portwenn team captain. However, Morwenna is forced to find a replacement rower when Eric dislocates his finger. Meanwhile, Mel Hendy goes to see Martin at the surgery worried about a rash that could be infectious, but she ends up opening up to him about her concerns over her husband's drinking. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa arrive home to find Chris Parsons waiting for them

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz, with six more contestants competing in Fastest Finger First to see who will sit in the hot seat and have the chance to take home the £1million jackpot. To make things easier, the players have four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal

programme.title: The Secret Mediterranean with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The broadcaster begins the final leg of his trip in Egypt, peering down through the waves at Cleopatra's sunken palace in Alexandria, where the country's last queen romanced Julius Caesar and Mark Antony. On the divided island of Cyprus, he gains rare access to a buffer zone established in 1974 by the UN and discovers settlements frozen in time. His journey ends in Malta with a chance to visit mysterious underground caves

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Sport Gives Back Awards
programme.desc: Jeanette Kwakye hosts the inaugural staging of the awards ceremony from the Royal Institution in London. The event celebrates and acknowledges the many charities, staff and volunteers that focus on improving the lives of others in the UK. The Sport Gives Back Awards is an evening dedicated to recognising those who have excelled in using sport and dance as a vehicle for social change

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his dealers are at The Cheltenham Racecourse, where Stewart Hofgartner comes across a fireman's helmet, and Alys Dobbie finds a dolls house. Meanwhile, Mark Stevens is mesmerised by a diamond ring and splashes out with plenty of cash to get it

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five friends from Brighton answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Paula, Steve, Carol and Tom pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie is at his lowest ebb and soon trapped, Harriet defends Malone, and Tracy supports Charity

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen phones Geoff with a surprising message, and Adam suspects that Gary has killed Rick. Nicky informs Daniel they could do more than talk for the money he's paying her

programme.title: Paul O'Grady for the Love of Dogs: What Happened Next
programme.desc: The comedian catches up with some of the most memorable dogs he has met from the eight years he has spent at Battersea Dogs and Cats Home, and learns how their lives have been transformed since leaving the shelter. In the first episode, there is a surprising revelation about Tulip, the puppy Paul fell in love with five years ago. Elsewhere, Paul discovers the uplifting story of Bruce, a terrified puggle who has gone on to change the lives of his new family

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Jodie's acting career gets a boost when she is accepted into stage school, but the high fees may be a problem, while Liam is back to visit his dad Les and is interested in a position at Blow & Go. Sir Henry asks Joyce if the Solana can host the Pride of Benidorm awards at short notice, but a double booking leads to a fight between two Peter Andre tribute acts and host Sammy Valentino starts causing trouble. Can a special star performance from Crystal save the day? Guest starring Shane Richie, Joan Collins and Kevin Bishop

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: The actress talks to Piers about her turbulent private life, which has seen her marry five times, and discusses the effect that roles such as Alexis in 1980s soap Dynasty has had on her career, which has spanned more than 60 films and dozens of TV series

programme.title: When Football Changed Forever
programme.desc: The dramatic story of the 1991/92 Football League Division One season - the final campaign before the Premier League began. Leeds United overcame fierce rivals Manchester United to emerge as champions, thanks to a certain Frenchman named Eric Cantona

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the dealers visit Warwick, where items of interest include a massive silver ring used for dishes and a 1960s classic car. Fay Rutter is tempted to take up windsurfing

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five prison officers from the north east answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. Highlights include a diver slapped by a stingray and a man who hits himself with an axe

programme.title: Eat, Shop, Save
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and her team of experts return to give more families an eight-week challenge to get fitter, eat better and save money on their shopping. First up are the Formstones from Pontefract in West Yorkshire, whose aim is to stop snacking, ditch the convenience foods and get their chaotic shopping habits under control

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Wales is the only country in the world with a designated footpath that stretches around its entire coastline. Sean Fletcher sets out to explore the entire 870-mile Wales Coast Path, which runs from Chepstow in the south to Queensferry in Flintshire in the north. His first leg takes Sean along the coast of north Wales and on to the island of Anglesey. Along the way searches for Britain's rarest toad, turns his hand to making a Bronze Age axe head and has a beach cookout using mussels and oysters caught fresh from the Menai Strait

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the doors of British factories to find out how they keep shelves stocked, from massive factories making meals in their millions to family firms trading on secret recipes. The first edition celebrates snacks, visiting the Wagon Wheel factory in south Wales making millions of biscuits a year and the Melton Mowbray company responsible for an award-winning pork pie. Plus, how potatoes are taken from the field and turned into crisps in a matter of hours

programme.title: Madeleine McCann: The Hunt for the Prime Suspect
programme.desc: Three-year-old Madeleine McCann disappeared from her parents' holiday apartment in Portugal 13 years ago, a mystery that has gripped the world. Now German prosecutors believe they have a prime suspect, a convicted sex offender who was living near the apartments at the time. But why has it taken a decade for him to be linked to the crime? Julie Etchingham investigates a trail that has involved police forces from at least three European countries

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unforgettable World Cup Goals
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a rundown of the best 50 goals from the FIFA World Cup, featuring memorable strikes from Michael Owen, Dennis Bergkamp, Diego Maradona and Pele among the contenders for the most unforgettable World Cup goal in the history of the tournament

programme.title: American Dad!

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian is devastated to learn his former girlfriend Jillian is planning to get married - until he meets The Hills star Lauren Conrad and begins to think she is his perfect partner

programme.title: American Dad!

Meanwhile, Steve and his friends stage wrestling matches in the basement, but things get out of hand when an uninvited participant shows up

programme.title: American Dad!

programme.title: The Cleveland Show

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.desc: Lois takes drastic action to save the life of Stewie's new best friend after his parents refuse treatment for his illness due to their religious beliefs. Meanwhile, Peter has a spiritual experience of his own. With the guest voice of Judy Greer (Archer)

programme.desc: Peter has a palm reading and discovers he led a past life as a gentleman in 17th-century England, where the fearsome Stewart III reigned. programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Brian are forced to attend a 12-step AA meeting to kick their drinking habits following a public disturbance. programme.title: American Dad!

programme.title: Bless This House

programme.desc: Dr Price and his wife hire a housekeeper, little realising that the woman is his long-lost mother

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz's past catches up with her, and Vernon's dreams of wealth go up in smoke. Meanwhile, Merton receives crime-fighting assistance from an unlikely source - two locals dressed as Batman and Robin, who have spotted criminal activity on a derelict farm

programme.desc: Marlon talks Mandy into dressing up as a Christmas fairy, Frank insists that Kim take a wedding gift to Dave and Kathy, and Biff asks Frank for time off for a honeymoon

programme.desc: Vic enjoys watching Viv squirm after her meal with the girls does not go as planned, and she tries to talk to Terry about their future. Tina tells Frank that Steve is jealous

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim cannot stop thinking about Liz and their wasted marriage. Jack arranges a pub outing to a racecourse. Mike tells Kevin he is selling the garage and gives him first refusal

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally is excited at the prospect of Kevin owning his own garage, and Josie is surprised to find how profitable it is. Steve and Andy ask Liz to retract her complaint against Jim

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz's past catches up with her, and Vernon's dreams of wealth go up in smoke. Meanwhile, Merton receives crime-fighting assistance from an unlikely source - two locals dressed as Batman and Robin, who have spotted criminal activity on a derelict farm

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A respected gynaecologist is accused of sexually assaulting a patient during a routine examination, but Brooks, Devlin and Chandler struggle to find any firm evidence and the team becomes divided. Crown Prosecutor Alesha Phillips refuses to accept defeat and takes on formidable defence barrister Phyllis Gladstone. Crime drama, guest starring Derek Riddell and Lesley Manville, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Harriet Walter and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part one of two. The badly burned body of a disabled man turns up in a remote part of Manchester and a few days later another corpse is discovered. The detectives start to realise the victims were not just murdered, but tortured too. They make an arrest, but it becomes clear their investigation has just scratched the surface. Meanwhile, Janet throws her husband out and Rachel's estranged brother turns up on her doorstep. With Lisa Riley and Pippa Haywood

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Downtrodden detective Jack Frost investigates after an unidentified man's body is found on top of a pile of glass at a council recycling centre - and the subsequent autopsy reveals a few surprises. Another case is soon on the cards when an apparent suicide raises suspicions. On the personal front, Frost decides to get in shape and joins a gym, where he falls for the charms of his fitness trainer. Starring David Jason and Claire Cox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.desc: A mysterious marksman sharpens up his sniper skills on a flock of unsuspecting sheep, before heading for Aidensfield and murdering Liz's ex-boyfriend, a newly arrived doctor. Drama, with Jason Durr making his final appearance as Mike Bradley

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric and Kathy reach a deal over the tearooms, Viv is nervous about going back to work at the post office, and Linda is scared when Chris pays her another surprise visit

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tina resigns from working for Frank, Zak is fed up with playing Santa Claus, Biff's stag night gets underway at the wine bar, and Kim has a shock proposal for Dave

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine moves back in with her parents, Maureen heads to Lowestoft as she cannot cope not hearing from Reg, and Vicky and Steve visit Jim in prison

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Eric tells Curly that Reg has resigned from his job. Mike urges Sally to push Kevin harder to buy the garage. Maureen fears for Reg's safety

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A mysterious marksman sharpens up his sniper skills on a flock of unsuspecting sheep, before heading for Aidensfield and murdering Liz's ex-boyfriend, a newly arrived doctor. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate a murder committed on the first night of a quiz weekend held at Chaucer College. Sealing the college grounds to interrogate the remaining 11 contestants, the pair discover the victim had a track record for sexual harassment involving fellow competitors, as well as past dealings with a shady engineering business funded by Russian gangsters and masterminded by Oxford professor Charles Milner - another quiz fan. With guest stars Alan Davies (QI), Timothy West (Bleak House) and Nicholas Farrell (The Iron Lady)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A tragic communication error between officers leaves gay police constable Nick Bentley caught in the crossfire of armed drug dealers, with no back-up. Brooks and Devlin follow a lead to a small-time dealer who claims to have seen a second policeman standing nearby, doing nothing while his partner bled to death. The story shocks the detectives and their boss Chandler as they investigate a homophobic religious group at the centre of the incident involving members of their own force. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Despite arresting four people, the detectives face a wall of silence in their investigation into the grisly murders - but they eventually make a breakthrough, which doesn't quite give them the answers they were expecting. On the domestic front, Rachel wakes up in bed with charismatic colleague Sean - a mistake she is keen never to repeat - and Janet wants to keep her newly single status a secret

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

Jean Alexander guest stars

programme.desc: Biff and Linda's wedding day arrives, Sandra Fowler is determined to stop her son's wedding, and an argument between Kim, Frank and Dave at Home Farm ends in tragedy

programme.desc: Mary's past catches up with her when an unwelcome visitor delivers an ultimatum, as secrets and rifts threaten the unity of the family. Final series of the costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Michelle Dockery

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when the body of missing 10-year-old Jodie Gaines is found in a skip. The pair immediately identify two main suspects - a taxi driver who claims to have dropped the girl off at her guitar lesson, and the teacher who swears she never arrived. The case then takes an unexpected turn, leaving Steel and Phillips with the toughest choices of their careers. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: It's Murray and Bailey this week, as Rachel travels to Bristol with the DCI, who is advising on a rape and murder case that bears similarities to one she worked on 13 years previously involving the deaths of four women. Back at home, meanwhile, the detective discovers her brother Dom has been prostituting himself in her flat - and also learns the charges against her ex-boyfriend Nick are being dropped

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Rural drama, starring Ruth Platt, Robert Hands, Mark Jordan and Craig Heaney

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When Sid receives a bonus, the family are quick to remind him how helpful they all are

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is left two large packing cases in an uncle's will - but they come with a terrible curse

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Dr Price and his wife hire a housekeeper, little realising that the woman is his long-lost mother

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz's past catches up with her, and Vernon's dreams of wealth go up in smoke. Meanwhile, Merton receives crime-fighting assistance from an unlikely source - two locals dressed as Batman and Robin, who have spotted criminal activity on a derelict farm

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon talks Mandy into dressing up as a Christmas fairy, Frank insists that Kim take a wedding gift to Dave and Kathy, and Biff asks Frank for time off for a honeymoon

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic enjoys watching Viv squirm after her meal with the girls does not go as planned, and she tries to talk to Terry about their future. Tina tells Frank that Steve is jealous

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim cannot stop thinking about Liz and their wasted marriage. Jack arranges a pub outing to a racecourse. Mike tells Kevin he is selling the garage and gives him first refusal

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally is excited at the prospect of Kevin owning his own garage, and Josie is surprised to find how profitable it is. Steve and Andy ask Liz to retract her complaint against Jim

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A respected gynaecologist is accused of sexually assaulting a patient during a routine examination, but Brooks, Devlin and Chandler struggle to find any firm evidence and the team becomes divided. Crown Prosecutor Alesha Phillips refuses to accept defeat and takes on formidable defence barrister Phyllis Gladstone. Crime drama, guest starring Derek Riddell and Lesley Manville, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Harriet Walter and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part one of two. The badly burned body of a disabled man turns up in a remote part of Manchester and a few days later another corpse is discovered. The detectives start to realise the victims were not just murdered, but tortured too. They make an arrest, but it becomes clear their investigation has just scratched the surface. Meanwhile, Janet throws her husband out and Rachel's estranged brother turns up on her doorstep. With Lisa Riley and Pippa Haywood

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Downtrodden detective Jack Frost investigates after an unidentified man's body is found on top of a pile of glass at a council recycling centre - and the subsequent autopsy reveals a few surprises. Another case is soon on the cards when an apparent suicide raises suspicions. On the personal front, Frost decides to get in shape and joins a gym, where he falls for the charms of his fitness trainer. Starring David Jason and Claire Cox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.desc: Sid informs Jean he has been given a temporary promotion at work, but she soon begins to doubt what he says. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.desc: Ida gets more than she bargained for during a visit to the fish and chip shop

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A mysterious marksman sharpens up his sniper skills on a flock of unsuspecting sheep, before heading for Aidensfield and murdering Liz's ex-boyfriend, a newly arrived doctor. While she attempts to comfort the victim's bereaved spouse, Bellamy is shot in the arm and sets out in pursuit of the rifle-toting villain. Drama, with Jason Durr making his final appearance as Mike Bradley

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric and Kathy reach a deal over the tearooms, Viv is nervous about going back to work at the post office, and Linda is scared when Chris pays her another surprise visit

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tina resigns from working for Frank, Zak is fed up with playing Santa Claus, Biff's stag night gets underway at the wine bar, and Kim has a shock proposal for Dave

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine moves back in with her parents, Maureen heads to Lowestoft as she cannot cope not hearing from Reg, and Vicky and Steve visit Jim in prison

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Eric tells Curly that Reg has resigned from his job. Mike urges Sally to push Kevin harder to buy the garage. Maureen fears for Reg's safety

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A mysterious marksman sharpens up his sniper skills on a flock of unsuspecting sheep, before heading for Aidensfield and murdering Liz's ex-boyfriend, a newly arrived doctor. While she attempts to comfort the victim's bereaved spouse, Bellamy is shot in the arm and sets out in pursuit of the rifle-toting villain. Drama, with Jason Durr making his final appearance as Mike Bradley

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate a murder committed on the first night of a quiz weekend held at Chaucer College. Sealing the college grounds to interrogate the remaining 11 contestants, the pair discover the victim had a track record for sexual harassment involving fellow competitors, as well as past dealings with a shady engineering business funded by Russian gangsters and masterminded by Oxford professor Charles Milner - another quiz fan. With guest stars Alan Davies (QI), Timothy West (Bleak House) and Nicholas Farrell (The Iron Lady)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A tragic communication error between officers leaves gay police constable Nick Bentley caught in the crossfire of armed drug dealers, with no back-up. Brooks and Devlin follow a lead to a small-time dealer who claims to have seen a second policeman standing nearby, doing nothing while his partner bled to death. The story shocks the detectives and their boss Chandler as they investigate a homophobic religious group at the centre of the incident involving members of their own force. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Despite arresting four people, the detectives face a wall of silence in their investigation into the grisly murders - but they eventually make a breakthrough, which doesn't quite give them the answers they were expecting. On the domestic front, Rachel wakes up in bed with charismatic colleague Sean - a mistake she is keen never to repeat - and Janet wants to keep her newly single status a secret

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield residents are up in arms over the noisy lorries rumbling through the village on their way to Jim Pinder's quarry, and David joins the protest, only to end up spending a night in the cells. Sheep are stolen from Lily Barton's land, and her neighbour promptly becomes the prime suspect - only for her farm to be set alight. Meanwhile, Bellamy strives to fan the flames of passion with Gina. Jean Alexander guest stars

programme.desc: Biff and Linda's wedding day arrives, Sandra Fowler is determined to stop her son's wedding, and an argument between Kim, Frank and Dave at Home Farm ends in tragedy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally tells Kevin they should ask Rita for a loan, competition heats up to buy the garage, and Maureen receives some horrifying news from Curly and Raquel

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel tries to make Maureen see that she is better off without Reg. Derek and Mavis look into acquiring an allotment. Kevin makes a decision about buying the garage

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Angie persuades Robert to arrange an evening out with the lads _ but neither she nor Ida are prepared for the consequences

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mary's past catches up with her when an unwelcome visitor delivers an ultimatum, as secrets and rifts threaten the unity of the family. News about the running of Downton Hospital puts Violet on the warpath, while those below stairs find social changes are putting their futures in jeopardy and Daisy's good intentions at the Mallerton House auction have disastrous consequences. Final series of the costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Michelle Dockery

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when the body of missing 10-year-old Jodie Gaines is found in a skip. The pair immediately identify two main suspects - a taxi driver who claims to have dropped the girl off at her guitar lesson, and the teacher who swears she never arrived. The case then takes an unexpected turn, leaving Steel and Phillips with the toughest choices of their careers. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: It's Murray and Bailey this week, as Rachel travels to Bristol with the DCI, who is advising on a rape and murder case that bears similarities to one she worked on 13 years previously involving the deaths of four women. Back at home, meanwhile, the detective discovers her brother Dom has been prostituting himself in her flat - and also learns the charges against her ex-boyfriend Nick are being dropped

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason

programme.desc: TV cameras descend on the wards to make a documentary featuring Dr Thorpe, but mayhem, chaos and disorder rear their ugly heads as the patients vie for attention. Hospital comedy, starring James Bolam, Peter Bowles and Richard Wilson

programme.desc: Templar poses as a Russian secret police chief to catch an assassin who has claimed the lives of 13 intelligence agents. Starring Roger Moore, Glynn Edwards and Burt Kwouk

programme.desc: The outlaws rescue King Richard the Lionheart from a gang of vagabonds - but his gratitude proves disappointingly short-lived. Drama, starring Michael Praed

programme.desc: Jim poses as a photographer to investigate a terrorist group's activities. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

However, Wilson's wife soon appears claiming her husband is unable to fight. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle teams up with a Hong Kong cop, who is in London trailing a Triad gang supplying drugs to US army personnel in Germany while operating an international protection racket. Meanwhile, Bodie goes undercover as a down-and-out in a squat where two young drug addicts have died. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan uses a criminal's abandoned dog as bait to lure his remorseful owner back into the country from France. However, associates of the villain spirit the hound away from its pound and a race begins as to who can locate the dog first. Alfred Marks, Brian Glover and Brian Wilde guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: A diamond trail leads Templar to mannequins and murder. Classic adventure, starring Roger Moore as globe-trotting adventurer Simon Templar

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Prince John arrives at Sherwood with a mysterious prisoner in tow, and the outlaws hatch a plan to rescue him - little suspecting the effect he will have on Marion. Fantasy drama, starring Michael Praed

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Jim goes undercover to infiltrate a drugs gang, but the operation runs into trouble when his partner tries to help out. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry protects a widowed garage owner from her grasping family and Arthur spots a chance to achieve one of his greatest ambitions - getting his hands on a Rolls-Royce. Lionel Jeffries and Keith Barron guest star alongside Dennis Waterman and George Cole

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates a consortium building a lethal fighter plane in eastern Europe, that appears willing to kill to supress publication of the aircraft's appalling safetly record. Lynda Bellingham guest stars, with Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2003 Relived
programme.desc: After brushing aside Georgia in their opening Pool C game at the 2003 Rugby World Cup, England faced a tougher challenge against the South Africans at the Subiaco Oval in Perth. The stakes were high - with the winner likely to secure a more favourable quarter-final draw - and with the sniping Joost van der Westhuizen pulling the strings from scrum-half for the Springboks, Clive Woodward's team needed to be on their mettle. Presented by Jim Rosenthal with Austin Healey, Will Carling and Francois Pienaar in the studio and commentary from John Taylor and Steve Smith

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.desc: For the final leg of their US jaunt, the trio arrive in Texas, a meat-lover's paradise where Gordon Ramsay is keen to introduce his companions to some of the best barbecue food known to man. In Lexington, Gordon turns lumberjack in an attempt to fell a dead oak - which is used to infuse ovens with wood smoke. There's a break for a bit of skinny dipping, before the trio fish go fishing, and stay in a strange cabin deep in the woods, surviving on beans and bourbon. They then hit the state capital of Austin, dressing as cowboys in the local bar and at the rodeo, before one last barbecue supper of slow-smoked beef brisket, pork ribs and spicy sausage

programme.title: Logan Lucky (2017)
programme.desc: Two brothers plan to pull off an ambitious heist during a Nascar race in North Carolina, but first they need the help of a convicted safe-cracker - who is currently doing time in prison. Steven Soderberg's crime caper, starring Channing Tatum, Adam Driver, Daniel Craig and Riley Keough

programme.desc: Terry gets a job chauffeuring the villainous Micky the Fish, but fears for his life when the gangster's mistress reports her jewels missing. Light-hearted crime caper, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are trained to act as bodyguards to a Middle Eastern president who is preparing to sign an important peace treaty. Meanwhile, two killers are receiving similar training to prepare them to assassinate him. As their paths cross, the CI5 operatives learn the would-be killers have some useful inside information and share more in common with them than they may have imagined

programme.title: Golf: PGA Tour Highlights
programme.desc: Extended highlights of The Memorial Tournament from the Muirfield Village Golf Course in Ohio, as the stars of the PGA Tour compete in one of the most high-profile events of the year, with a prize fund of more than $9million on offer

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2003 Relived
programme.desc: After a crucial win against South Africa, Clive Woodward's England side knew that victory against Samoa in Melbourne would all but secure top spot in Pool C. With evergreen prop Jason Leonard leading from the front row, skipper Martin Johnson in prime form and with a hugely talented backline including Jonny Wilkinson and Jason Robinson, England were expected to win comfortably, but Samoa had other ideas. Presented by Jim Rosenthal alongside Will Carling, Nigel Melville and Francois Pienaar in the studio with commentary from Nigel Starmer-Smith and Steve Smith

programme.title: The Outlaw Josey Wales (1976)
programme.desc: A Confederate soldier refuses to surrender at the end of the Civil War, and is branded a dangerous outlaw. Crossing America in search of sanctuary from the army and bounty hunters, he only wants solitude - but ends up with a strange assortment of travelling companions. Western, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood. With Chief Dan George and Sondra Locke

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2003 Relived
programme.desc: After brushing aside Georgia in their opening Pool C game at the 2003 Rugby World Cup, England faced a tougher challenge against the South Africans at the Subiaco Oval in Perth. The stakes were high - with the winner likely to secure a more favourable quarter-final draw - and with the sniping Joost van der Westhuizen pulling the strings from scrum-half for the Springboks, Clive Woodward's team needed to be on their mettle. Presented by Jim Rosenthal with Austin Healey, Will Carling and Francois Pienaar in the studio and commentary from John Taylor and Steve Smith

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.desc: For the final leg of their US jaunt, the trio arrive in Texas, a meat-lover's paradise where Gordon Ramsay is keen to introduce his companions to some of the best barbecue food known to man. In Lexington, Gordon turns lumberjack in an attempt to fell a dead oak - which is used to infuse ovens with wood smoke. There's a break for a bit of skinny dipping, before the trio fish go fishing, and stay in a strange cabin deep in the woods, surviving on beans and bourbon. They then hit the state capital of Austin, dressing as cowboys in the local bar and at the rodeo, before one last barbecue supper of slow-smoked beef brisket, pork ribs and spicy sausage

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2003 Relived
programme.desc: After a crucial win against South Africa, Clive Woodward's England side knew that victory against Samoa in Melbourne would all but secure top spot in Pool C. With evergreen prop Jason Leonard leading from the front row, skipper Martin Johnson in prime form and with a hugely talented backline including Jonny Wilkinson and Jason Robinson, England were expected to win comfortably, but Samoa had other ideas. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Last to host in Yeovil is 50-year-old used-car dealer Andy Neil, who is supremely confident that his fine dining experience, along with the 'beautiful' surroundings of his home, will bag him the £1,000 prize. Six amuse-bouches for his starter are a visual and culinary treat, but his constant questions and need for feedback and praise threaten to put off his fellow diners. programme.title: Witches: A Century of Murder
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb's investigation into the notorious 17th-century witch trials concludes with a profile of Matthew Hopkins, the opportunistic puritan witch-hunter who transformed paranoia about Satanism into a lucrative business as he hanged hundreds of people for crimes they could not possibly have committed. Making use of contemporary reports, Suzannah paints a portrait of a man who believed he could cross any boundary and break any law to achieve his goals. Naming himself 'Witchfinder General', Hopkins toured the country charging fees for his services, but Suzannah's research reveals that ultimately, the public's distaste for witches was not as strong as its hatred of being conned by charlatans. Hopkins would eventually die of tuberculosis in relative obscurity, but his malign legacy lived on across the Atlantic, as witch-hunts began in Salem, Massachusetts

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: The life and work of Pieter Bruegel the Elder, generally considered the greatest Flemish painter of the 16th century, whose works remain among the most distinctive examples of Renaissance art, combining gritty humour with political comment. Tim Marlow reveals how Bruegel was influenced by the period of immense social change in which he lived

programme.title: Walking Britain's Lost Railways
programme.desc: Rob Bell explores more abandoned rail routes, beginning with one that cut an outrageous path through the dramatic hills and gorges of the Peak District - part of the 1860s express route between London and Manchester. From Matlock to the spa town splendour of Buxton, Rob unearths a tale of Victorian ambition, as he travels along a route filled with great engineering, which is now a beloved part of the Peak District National Park

programme.title: The Railway 24/7
programme.desc: As coronavirus spreads across Europe, passengers start to abandon the country's railways, and bosses at Manchester HQ call an emergency meeting to discuss the impact the pandemic will have on their services, and how they can keep trains running for the sake of key workers. As the UK lockdown begins, passenger numbers plummet by 95% and revenue falls at an alarming rate

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Police from Derbyshire are on a campaign to round up wanted criminals on the run, and officer Matt Copestake joins a high-speed pursuit of a man wanted across two counties. North of Chesterfield, Andy Swift receives intel on the whereabouts of car linked to a wanted suspect, and units from across the county close in on the suspect car and its occupants

programme.title: Shoplifters: At War with the Law
programme.desc: Security guards in West Orchards shopping centre in Coventry try to tackle the issue of serial shoplifters, who are behind around 70% of all the thefts in the centre. Meanwhile at Weston Favell Shopping Centre in Northampton, the security team have their eye on a duo suspected of repeatedly shoplifting using young children as decoys

programme.title: Sarah Payne: 5 Mistakes That Caught a Killer
programme.desc: Examining the murder of Sarah Payne, an eight-year-old girl who was killed by Roy Whiting in 2000, and the mistakes he made that led to him being arrested and locked up for life. The programme features interviews with detectives, forensic experts and other crucial witnesses, charting the police investigation and the evidence that led them to her killer

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: Between 1975 and 1980, serial killer Peter Sutcliffe - known in the press as the Yorkshire Ripper - murdered 13 women and attacked at least seven more in the north of England. In this programme, psychologists and criminology experts analyse the personality of Sutcliffe and look at events in his childhood and adult life that may have triggered his descent into murder, and also examine how he managed to avoid suspicion despite being interviewed by police numerous times

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben travels to Cambodia to live with a fellow Brit who is raising his own castaway tribe, joining his renegade host as he defends the precious sea-life around him. They go diving to see the wildlife he is trying to protect, and on night patrol to ward off illegal fishermen, discovering island bunkers from the brutal Khmer Rouge regime, and experiencing how fragile life is for this family

programme.title: The Funny Thing About
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas is joined by celebrities and comedians for a talking heads show combining observational comedy, confessions and anecdotes. programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow charts the life of Greek artist Domenicos Theotocopolous, dubbed El Greco, one of the most distinctive painters of the 16th century. His work was considered to be ahead of its time - so much so that it was all but ignored for 300 years. El Greco developed a highly individualistic style that eventually proved enormously influential, paving the way for the Baroque and providing the inspiration for 20th-century expressionism

programme.title: Neighbours

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Bella's newfound independence inspires everyone, and Dean summons Ziggy for back up as tensions between Maggie and Ben continue to escalate

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris explores five countries that were part of Yugoslavia, which had an extensive state-run railway network before its break-up in the 1990s and the Balkan Wars of Independence that followed. From Slovenia, Chris heads east along an old Austro-Hungarian railway that was once used by the Orient Express, pays a visit to a very unusual railway-related battlefield in Bosnia and rides on one of the world's great mountain railways on his way to the Serbian capital Belgrade before finishing his journey in Montenegro

programme.title: Then & Now
programme.desc: The Mersey is one of Britain's most magnificent rivers, running 70 miles from Stockport to the Irish Sea. This documentary reveals how the waterway has been at the heart of British shipbuilding for more than 180 years, from the first aircraft carrier HMS Ark Royal to the latest, HMS Prince of Wales. It has also been important for business, with 40 per cent of all world trade coming through Liverpool's docks in the 19th century, and played a key role in the Allied victory during the Second World War

programme.title: The Curse of King Tut
programme.desc: The 1922 discovery of Tutankhamun's tomb in Egypt's Valley of Kings remains one of the greatest archaeological finds of all time. But when Howard Carter broke through the sealed entrance to reveal thousands of priceless artefacts and the teenage body of the boy king, rumours of the Pharaoh's revenge consumed the media. And when Carter's benefactor Lord Carnarvon suddenly died, the belief that a deadly curse had been unleashed ran like wildfire. This documentary looks back at Carter and Carnarvon's discovery, and explores the truth behind the stories of the curse

programme.title: Concorde: 120 Seconds to Disaster - 20 Years On
programme.desc: The incident from July 2000 when an Air France Concorde crashed shortly after take-off from Charles de Gaulle Airport. All 109 passengers and crew members on board were killed, as well as at least four people on the ground. The entire world reeled in shock as in 24 years of commercial flight, Concorde had an unblemished record and was considered to be one of the safest passenger planes in history. This documentary re-examines the causes of the incident, as revealed by French and British air crash investigators

programme.title: Sinkholes: Buried Underground
programme.desc: Cameras follow some of the people who have seen their houses and lives destroyed by the terrifying phenomenon of sinkholes

programme.title: Greatest Ever TV Blunders
programme.desc: Elis James introduces outtakes from popular TV shows, including classic continuity cock-ups and bungles from news shows

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Bill Nighy narrates a trip through Wales' highest peaks and deepest valleys. Starting in Pwllheli in the north west, the route heads along the lines that were built during the slate industry's heyday. At Porthmadog, the journey briefly switches to the reconstructed steam trains of the Welsh Highland National Heritage Railway to Minffordd station. Nearby is the village of Portmeirion, famous for its role in the classic TV series The Prisoner. Rejoining the Cambrian Line, local farmers reveal what the railway means to them, before the route takes in the seaside resort of Aberdovey

programme.title: World's Most Pampered Pets
programme.desc: Documentary looking for the animal with the most lavish lifestyle and revealing why some owners spend as much money on their pets as they do on themselves. American reality TV star Aubrey O'Day takes her pampered pooches for an extreme makeover, including purple highlights for one and a pink Mohican for another, while Louise Harris from Essex auditions Britain's best-looking 'diva dogs' for a new fashion line. Also featured are the world's most expensive pet accessories and the sad story of Kalu the chimp - who missed out on an inheritance of £40million when its owner revealed she had lost her entire fortune

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow explores the life and work of 17th-century artist Peter Paul Rubens, who enjoyed an active and eventful career. He was regarded as the chief exponent of the Baroque style, merging the grace of the Italian High Renaissance with the realism and landscapes of the northern tradition, and was perhaps best known for painting voluptuous female nudes within large-scale mythological allegories

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice offers advice to another would-be seller, this time visiting a house in Ashover Hay, Derbyshire

programme.title: The Great Sioux Massacre (1965)
programme.desc: A corrupt senator coaxes Colonel Custer out of retirement with the prospect of presidential office, and the soldier tries to further his political ambitions by wresting control of the current military campaign from its commanding officer, culminating in his last stand at the Battle of the Little Bighorn. Western, with Philip Carey, Joseph Cotten, Darren McGavin and Julie Sommars

programme.title: Freedom Radio (1940)
programme.desc: A Viennese doctor tries to maintain business as usual while living under Nazi rule. However, events take a sickening turn when his wife begins wholeheartedly espousing Hitler's views - prompting him to start broadcasting Allied propaganda from a secret transmitter. Second World War drama, starring Clive Brook, Diana Wynyard, Raymond Huntley and Derek Farr

programme.title: Bitter Victory (1957)
programme.desc: Two British officers are assigned to lead a raid on a German stronghold in Libya and steal secret documents. However, one of the pair suspects the other of having had an affair with his wife and the tension between them threatens the mission. Second World War drama, starring Richard Burton, Curt Jurgens, Ruth Roman and Christopher Lee

programme.title: The Man from the Alamo (1953)
programme.desc: A soldier serving at the battle of the Alamo is sent to warn his comrades' families of the impending danger, but ends up falsely branded a deserter. He tries to clear his name with acts of heroism, including tracking down the traitor who robbed the casualties after the battle was over. Western, starring Glenn Ford and Julia Adams

programme.title: The Iron Lady Interview Special
programme.desc: Meryl Streep discusses her Oscar-winning performance as Margaret Thatcher

programme.title: Eddie the Eagle (2016)
programme.desc: Biopic of British sporting underdog Eddie Edwards. Determined to fulfil his dreams of becoming an Olympic athlete, Edwards trains as a ski jumper - a field in which Britain had no other competitors. He secures a place in the 1988 Winter Olympics, where his can-do attitude makes him a media sensation in the face of a losing streak. Starring Taron Egerton, Hugh Jackman and Christopher Walken

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Rogue Nation (2015)
programme.desc: The team of secret agents faces being shut down when the head of the CIA convinces the US Senate that it is too dangerous. The operatives of the Impossible Mission Force become fugitives when they defy orders and plot to bring down a shadowy society of assassins. Spy thriller sequel, starring Tom Cruise, Jeremy Renner, Rebecca Ferguson and Simon Pegg

programme.title: I Am Not a Witch (2017)
programme.desc: In a rural Zambian village, Shula, a taciturn, serious-looking pre-teen girl, is accused of witchcraft on the shaky basis that strange things have happened since she turned up out of the blue. But when Shula refuses to confirm or deny whether she's a witch, police officer Josephine contacts government official Mr Banda, who agrees to take care of her. Drama, starring Maggie Mulubwa and Nellie Munamonga

programme.title: Accidental Love (2015)
programme.desc: A small town waitress suffers a freak accident that causes her to act erratically. Unable to afford medical treatment, she journeys to Washington DC in a bid to raise awareness of her plight. Once there, she bumps into a bumbling but well-meaning young politician who promises to help her. Romantic comedy, starring Jessica Biel, Jake Gyllenhaal and Catherine Keener

programme.title: Hell Below Zero (1954)
programme.desc: A thrill-seeking explorer joins a whaling ship and is persuaded by the female captain to investigate the disappearance of her father on an earlier expedition. The pair become suspicious of her former lover - and are proved right when he leaves them for dead in Antarctic waters. Action adventure, starring Alan Ladd, Joan Tetzel, Basil Sydney and Stanley Baker

programme.title: The Gunfight at Dodge City (1959)
programme.desc: A gambler becomes the sheriff of a frontier town, planning to use his position to avenge his brother's murderers - but the arrival of a face from the past muddies the water, and leaves him doubting the identity of the killer. Western, starring Joel McCrea, Julie Adams, John McIntire, Nancy Gates and Richard Anderson

programme.title: The Iron Lady Interview Special
programme.desc: Meryl Streep discusses her Oscar-winning performance as Margaret Thatcher

programme.title: Our Man in Havana (1959)
programme.desc: A vacuum cleaner salesman is recruited as an agent by British intelligence, but struggles to cope with the demands of the role and starts to fabricate information to keep himself gainfully employed. Carol Reed's satirical spy drama, based on Graham Greene's novel, starring Alec Guinness, Ralph Richardson, Maureen O'Hara, Noel Coward and Burl Ives

programme.title: Invitation to a Gunfighter (1964)
programme.desc: A Civil War veteran returns home to find a ruthless landowner has sold the family house and his land has been confiscated as so-called enemy property. When the soldier retaliates by breaking into his former home and barricading himself in, the land baron hires a mysterious gunslinger to force the rebel out - but once unleashed, the hired gun has ideas of his own. Western, starring Yul Brynner, George Segal, Janice Rule and Pat Hingle

programme.title: The Iron Lady Interview Special
programme.desc: Meryl Streep discusses her Oscar-winning performance as Margaret Thatcher

programme.title: The Iron Lady (2011)
programme.desc: The elderly Margaret Thatcher, now living in seclusion, tries to come to terms with the death of her husband Denis, bringing back memories of her controversial political career. She reflects on her rise to power in the male-dominated Conservative Party and the tumultuous events during her time in office. Biopic, starring an Oscar-winning Meryl Streep. With Jim Broadbent and Olivia Colman

programme.title: Snatched (2017)
programme.desc: After being dumped by her boyfriend before their holiday, Emily Middleton takes her cautious mother Linda with her to Ecuador. However, their lives are soon turned upside down when the pair are kidnapped. Fish-out-of-water comedy, starring Amy Schumer, Goldie Hawn and Joan Cusack

programme.title: Rough Night (2017)

programme.desc: Comedy outlining the seemingly random and fragile nature of the connections that can lead to people becoming part of a couple, showing the final stages of a number of relationships then flashing back to how they got together. Julian Barratt, Kerry Fox and Paul McGann star

programme.title: The Tall Stranger (1957)
programme.desc: A Union soldier struggles to recover from a violent encounter with a gang of lawless cattle rustlers, but his recuperation is cut short as he works to ensure the safety of a group of Confederate homesteaders journeying north at the end of the Civil War. Western based on a novel by Louis L'Amour, starring Joel McCrea, Virginia Mayo, Michael Ansara and Michael Pate

programme.title: Pursued (1947)
programme.desc: A brooding cowboy yearns to find peace, but his life seems to follow a tragic path because of the actions and hatred of a mysterious one-armed stranger and a forgotten incident from his childhood. Western, starring Robert Mitchum, Teresa Wright, Dean Jagger, Judith Anderson, Alan Hale and John Rodney

programme.title: The Naked Jungle (1954)
programme.desc: A tough South American cocoa plantation owner and his newly arrived mail-order bride are menaced by a huge column of ferocious killer ants destroying everything in their path. Thriller, starring Charlton Heston, Eleanor Parker and William Conrad

programme.title: Man in the Saddle (1951)
programme.desc: A wealthy and ambitious cattleman plans to seize all the land surrounding his own. A peaceful rancher living nearby ignores warnings to sell up and avoid a conflict, and as he suffers a campaign of intimidation, he is forced to adopt violent methods to defend his livelihood. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Alexander Knox, Joan Leslie and Ellen Drew

programme.desc: A kingdom stands on the brink of war when it is invaded by a vast horde of creatures who have travelled through a portal from their dying world. As dark magic works its influence on the world, two leaders from either side are set on a collision course with each other as they strive to protect their people. Fantasy adventure based on the best-selling computer game, starring Travis Fimmel, Paula Patton and Ben Foster

programme.title: Sweet Country (2017)
programme.desc: An aged Aboriginal farmhand shoots a white man in self defence and goes on the run as a posse gathers to hunt him down. Australian period drama, starring Hamilton Morris, Bryan Brown and Sam Neill

programme.title: The Business (2005)
programme.desc: A criminal flees London in the 1980s to start a new life on the Costa del Sol. He is soon drawn into the inner circle of a British gangster living in exile, but his relationship with a psychotic crook's girlfriend threatens to bring his criminal career to an abrupt end. Crime drama, starring Danny Dyer, Tamer Hassan and Geoff Bell

programme.desc: Sheldon is flattered when a graduate student takes a shine to him, but she thinks his social life is distracting him from his work and removes him from his limited circle of friends by forcing him to work on a troublesome scientific problem. Guest starring Riki Lindhome

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny's TV breaks down, so she heads to the boys' flat to watch her favourite shows. However, Sheldon tires of her presence and bans future visits, leading to an embarrassing form of retaliation. Meanwhile, Howard and Raj search for the America's Next Top Model house

programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. However, there is some grumbling over breakfast about the bacon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners stay at Compton House in Newark, Nottinghamshire, which is run by Lisa Holloway - who prides herself on providing a home away from home for her visitors. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave and Jo Bate welcome their guests to the White Horse, a pub and B&B in Ripon, North Yorkshire, where they hope an afternoon of morris dancing in front of the cathedral will make a lasting impression. Unfortunately, Ed and Jane Mitchell get locked inside their room by mistake, and there are arguments over tomatoes at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Retired teachers Ed and Jane Mitchell welcome the contestants to the Old Farmhouse, Cheshire, but everyone is shocked to discover they will have to share a bathroom. Meanwhile, Sam and Ian Sugden find an unwelcome surprise underneath their bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day arrives, as the guests gather to find out who has won and to thrash out their differences - from some fishy findings to a reminder of why sorry can be the hardest word. Later on, a heated exchange about cleaning ends in tears

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester's Penguin Island is home to one of the most enduring love stories in the zoo. At 22, Rud is the oldest on the island, and has been with his partner, Spike, for over 15 years. However, keepers are concerned that Rud's arthritic hip could be affecting his quality of life. Meanwhile, the Sumatran tigers are upgrading to a luxury new enclosure on the other side of the zoo as part of Chester's huge expansion. But they've lived in their home for most of their lives so it's not going to be an easy move

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Senior neurologist Dr Colin Driver comes up with an unconventional solution to treat a nurse's cat, which has been rushed into the surgery after being poisoned. The owners of a rare Polish sheepdog with a failed fracture repair on a broken leg are faced with the option of having the leg removed, or trying an innovative total elbow replacement. Plus, a self-confessed 'crazy cat lady' brings in a ginger tom which urgently needs leg surgery

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge helps Donna McDougal, who believes a horde of ancient armour was hidden in her chateau's lake. New parents in the Dordogne juggle the responsibility of looking after their baby with converting an attic into a bed-and-breakfast suite, and Limoges chateau owners Colette and Paul launch an ambitious new menu

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of musician Toby Leeming as he sets out to build a house wrapped around an ancient oak tree in rural Suffolk. The design for Toby's five-building home contains six bedrooms, six bathrooms, a music studio and an annexe - a vast project more like a village than a house. Creating this life changing vision on a £530k budget, and in only eight months, will not be an easy task, especially as they must build up close to an ancient oak without destroying any of the roots

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores Cardiff's Edwardian past, discovering how the coal industry transformed the Rhondda Valley in the early 1900s. She investigates the difficult and often dangerous work of the miners, and visits the trading floor where the first ever million pound cheque was signed. The programme also investigates how global shipping routes and plentiful employment brought immigrants from across the world to Wales, and meets descendants of people who came to the city in the early years of the 20th century

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Some of the cases paramedics are faced with when the holiday season is in full swing. In Nuneaton, the team is called to a 17-year-old who has been injured on a rugby pitch

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Bill Bailey taking on special guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Isy Suttie. Susie Dent is joined by Joe Lycett in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The air ambulance teams battle to save cyclist Graham's partially amputated leg, while Rose is freed from a rolled car in Buckinghamshire. Gary suffers a rare and extremely serious complete knee dislocation playing football, but strong pain relief results in some unexpected hilarity

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps self-builders Mick and Sally Wood construct a family home on the banks of the Humber. The couple have inherited a Grade II listed manor house and plan on renovating barns round the back. It's a big job on a small budget, and Charlie is concerned they'll never finish

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Eagle Rock in California to try to change the fortunes of Capri, a grubby restaurant which often remains closed during lunchtime because of the twin owners' lazy attitude. A crisis ensues as soon as Gordon Ramsay instructs the duo to clean the place, revealing that only tough measures are likely to save the business

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty finds out if eating too many poppy seeds could make him fail a drugs test, Kate Quilton learns the consequences of tipping waste cooking oil down the drain, and Matt Tebbutt has a Sicilian bake-off with an increasingly popular ancient grain bread

programme.title: Teleshopping
