programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Together with children from Redhills Community Primary School, Devon, Kirstie Allsopp makes a scarecrow for her local county show at Chagford. She also learns a thing or two from 14-year-old Sarah Whitely about how to show a saddleback pig, and she judges some of the cutest and shortest sheep handlers, aged 6 to 12

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps Irene Evans search for a home in western Andalusia, Spain, within commuting distance of Gibraltar, where she hopes to find work as a paralegal. She has decided to leave the UK after a tumultuous few years and has a budget of £90,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo restores a pair of designer chairs, while Henry Cole breathes new life into a decrepit garden table, transforming it into a colourful and valuable new one

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien turn an old, unloved filing cabinet into a valuable and desirable storage unit and convert a Bakelite voltmeter into a retro Bluetooth speaker

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge helps Donna McDougal, who believes a horde of ancient armour was hidden in her chateau's lake. New parents in the Dordogne juggle the responsibility of looking after their baby with converting an attic into a bed-and-breakfast suite, and Limoges chateau owners Colette and Paul launch an ambitious new menu

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton travels to Cornwall to help a couple who are building a new home in the back garden of their old one, complete with space to store a collection of motorcycles

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Knockaloe Beg Farm on the Isle of Man, where owners Fiona and John Anderson hope their guests will embrace their green ethos. They kick things off with an afternoon of laser mayhem, and continue the fun the next day by following breakfast with hands-on animal antics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests head to the Arches on the Isle of Man for their second stay, where owner Phil Routledge hopes to wow them with scenic views and scuba diving at the B&B's own pool. However, some of the owners are dismayed by modern gadgets with a lack of instructions, and even find cobwebs in their rooms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants arrive at the Trafford in Blackpool, owned by Robbie and John Welling, who are proud of their traditional 'bucket and spade' B&B. The guests are pleasantly surprised, even if previous host Phil is intent on finding dust as a bit of tit-for-tat retaliation, and the afternoon is spent learning the art of making sticks of rock

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Host Julie welcomes the contestants to Sheepy Lodge, Warwickshire, where they are shocked by their surroundings, and disappointed by a variety of things in the rooms. The host puts on some old-fashioned parlour games, before a fierce hide-and-seek contest erupts. At breakfast, Julie frets when she must prepare poached eggs, and is left emotional after the feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rival B&B owners meet to settle some scores and discover what they have been paid. Knockaloe Beg Farm owners Fiona and John Anderson tackle criticisms of a missing host at breakfast, and Phil Routledge from The Arches on the Isle of Man takes on Robbie and John Welling from The Trafford in Blackpool about their 'forensic investigation'. After some heated discussion, the winner is announced

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Rothschild giraffe Orla is about to give birth to her second calf but is three weeks overdue. This episode captures the arrival of the giraffe family's youngest member and its introduction to the rest of the herd. Cameras also follow the Chester team as they attempt to save mountain chicken frogs from extinction, and there's a look at the secret world of three disruptive aardvarks

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Staffordshire bull terrier requires attention when it suffers complete paralysis in its back legs, and a Devon rex cat is brought in after being struck by a car. Its eight-year-old owner faces a tough decision when X-rays reveal a severely broken pelvis and a ruptured bladder. Plus, a young cavalier king has had seven operations in a bid to fix its knee joint, and Noel tries to resolve the problem

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Donna McDougal's search for the armour in the lake continues. Tim and Krys, with the help of Tim's brother Ben, continue in their struggle to get the attic converted in time for the first guests of the season. Dick and his mother-in-law Jenny struggle with preparing and freezing 227kg of pork, before attempting to turn the final 2kg into salamis

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: For most people, living in a lighthouse is pure fantasy. Not so for Edward and Hazel Short and their two young daughters. Back in 2012, they set out to build a brand new, art deco, shining white lighthouse tower with additional bedrooms, guest rooms, and infinity pool on the side - all on a rugged but beautiful clifftop in North Devon. Kevin McCloud sees if they made that dream a reality

programme.title: 24 Hour Baby Hospital
programme.desc: Ecaterina is rushed in as her fifth child is in a hurry to make an appearance, and Ciara and Mick must come to terms with the news that one of their unborn twins will not survive

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager has a life-threatening bleed on the brain and requires emergency surgery, a 10-year-old gymnast sustains an injury to her wrist after performing a back bend, and a girl is brought in to the urgent care centre, needing treatment for her swollen tonsils. As they undergo treatment, their parents share stories of family life

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 75-year-old man who fell out of bed is suspected of having a stroke, and doctors have only a short window of time to administer a life-saving, clot-busting drug. A mother of two falls off her horse, and a six-year-old girl has a possible neck injury after an accident in the playground. Family members of the patients share their personal stories

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show, with Sean Lock and Henning Wehn taking on guest captain Bill Bailey and Jessica Hynes. Susie Dent is joined by Tom Allen in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: In Cornwall, Charlie Luxton helps Shelton and Jayne Grigg build a new home in the back garden of their old one. It's set to be a large, four-bed family home, with a huge kitchen-diner, and an enormous garage for Shelton's collection of classic motorcycles. However, an unexpected turn of events leaves the couple homeless and piles pressure on Shelton to get the new house finished

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to Wiltshire to help first-time self-builders Vince and Jackie Macey create a super-sustainable, three-bedroom house. Electrician Vince has some firm ideas on how things should be done, but unexpected setbacks threaten to ruin things, as they struggle to sell their old house and face running out of cash

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Dunsandles Guest House in New Brighton, Merseyside, where owner Alan Johnson and his professional sidekick Dave Owen hope to win the title - but a scandal with some unwanted hair on the bed sheets is not a good start, and two guests feel they are making an exhibition of themselves with a tea-trolley in full view of the neighbours

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the second day of the competition, the contestants visit the Lady Teal Hotel Boat on the waterways near Burnley in Lancashire, and owners Nick and Gina Mead hope to impress them with an afternoon of entertainment out on the locks. At breakfast, one of the guests admits to not enjoying his stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From York House in Tetbury, Gloucestershire, where Brock Bergius hopes that his cheeky sense of humour and perfectionist nature will help him win the competition. He is in his element making saucy jokes during an afternoon of sausage making, but his reluctance to stray from the menu at breakfast leaves some of his guests disappointed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Heyford House in Oxfordshire, where Leo and Sonya Brooke-Little hope to impress the contestants with their hospitality. The afternoon event is boxing, which Brock Burgius sees as the perfect opportunity to settle some scores with his fellow competitors

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's winner is announced as the B&B owners meet for the last time to thrash out their differences. Feedback includes criticism of a badly positioned tea tray, controversy over a hot chocolate and accusations of overenthusiastic hosting

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The Hi Way elephants have been hit with a potentially deadly virus, with the youngest members of the family most at risk, and the keepers and Chester Zoo's veterinary team do all they can to combat the mysterious illness. Meanwhile, Mac the stallion zebra is introduced to the zoo's three mares in the hope they will produce much needed offspring for this endangered species. There's also a look into the bizarre world of the naked mole rat

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick pushes the boundaries of veterinary medicine, and treats a German shepherd puppy that needs surgery on his hips and front leg using groundbreaking cell treatment. The vet must also perform delicate surgery on a chihuahua suffering from a neck deformity compressing its spinal cord - however, one false move could be fatal. Plus, a rescue dog with deformed front legs is brought into the surgery

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: In the Pays de la Loire, twenty-something couple Billy and Gwendoline want to open their chateau as a wedding location, but it's not going to plan as the chimney seems to have caught fire. Near Dijon, Clive and Tanith run an upmarket chateau hotel but need to win over the locals, so they put on an open day. Meanwhile, sisters Tanya and Marian prepare to visit their chateau in the Loire Valley. They need to get it ready for the first guests of the summer in less than a week's time, but are faced with everything from dead flies to leaking swimming pools

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits a home built on an airfield in Strathaven in Scotland to check on the progress made by Colin MacKinnon and his partner Marta Briongos. Their ambitious plan was to build a metal-sculptural home inspired by aircraft hangars right next to the runway, but the difficulties of building their elegant design saw a year-long project stretch to three years

programme.title: Outlander

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark, 32, is brought to resus with a head injury after having an epileptic seizure, and he is sent for an urgent CT scan to assess the damage to his brain. Anastasia, 80, comes to urgent care with a pain in her leg and while she is sent for an X-ray to rule out a blood clot, she shares the story of her arrival to study in England from Nigeria, only for civil war to break out back home. programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Russia as the motoring magazine returns, taking part in a race across the urban landscape of St Petersburg. James defends the honour of the car in a very small Renault, Richard tries to prove that pedalling is faster by riding a bicycle, while Jeremy attempts to demonstrate that water is the quickest way across town by piloting a hovercraft. To make matters more complicated, all three presenters are racing not only one another but also the Stig, who is making his way toward the finish line by public transport. Plus, the new Lamborghini Huracan is put through its paces and singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran takes to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.desc: The presenters embark on a road trip across Australia's Northern Territory in fast, luxurious GT cars, with Jeremy Clarkson choosing a BMW M6 Gran Coupe, Richard Hammond picking a Bentley Continental GT V8 and James May opting for a Nissan GT-R. The trio set out on an adventure that takes in crocodile-infested rivers, a race around a vast opencast mine and an encounter with one of the world's longest lorries, concluding with an unusual challenge involving a farm, their cars and 4,000 cows. Plus, 24 star Kiefer Sutherland takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Claustrophobic prison cells that contain a story worthy of international acclaim, and a vast mountainside complex that held the power to change the world

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: The team acquire a personalised Victorian pistol and try to track down the original owner. A customer brings in brasses that his dad brought back from the Somme

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The Jewish group the Avengers who, as the Second World War was ending, hunted down persecutors known to have contributed to deaths during the Holocaust, including Wilhelm Stuckart

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Senate House in Bloomsbury, Britain's oldest skyscraper and administrative centre of the University of London. Items under valuation include the hoof of a horse that fought in the Battle of Balaclava, a collection of stained glass panels that were recovered from a skip, and a diamond and platinum bracelet

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Claustrophobic prison cells that contain a story worthy of international acclaim, and a vast mountainside complex that held the power to change the world

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey to London from Buxton, Derbyshire, where he discovers how railways inspired a building craze and boosted tourism. In the Peak District, he joins a team of volunteers repairing damage done to ancient peat landscapes during the Industrial Revolution, and near the quarries of Stanton Moor he learns how trains transported Derbyshire's famous sandstone all over England

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the second leg of his journey from Buxton to London, stopping at Cromford, Derbyshire, where Bradshaw's railway guide reports Richard Arkwright built his original cotton mill. He also visits Derby to take a walk through Britain's first public park, and heads for Burton-on-Trent for a tour of Burton Bridges Breweries

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head for the attractive gardens of Trelissick, near Truro in Cornwall. Objects under examination include a bust of Winston Churchill found at the bottom of a lake and a group of medals owned by a proud grandson. A lifebelt tells the graphic story of a shipwreck off the Lizard peninsula in which the crew were rescued in desperate circumstances

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: Footage of animals that have unusual means of finding their way around. Dung beetles are able to navigate by the sky, using the position of the sun, moon and stars to determine which is the right direction to go in, while pigeons display remarkable powers of memory, able to recall complex travel routes and follow manmade roads and hedgerows

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The naturalist explores the extremes of birth in the animal kingdom, explaining why the giant panda gives birth to the smallest babies of any existing mammal. He also looks at the kiwi, asking why the bird produces a single egg that is a quarter of its body mass - one of the largest in the world - and almost too big to lay

programme.title: Warbird Workshop
programme.desc: A US Army Air Force C47 that dropped paratroopers on D-Day is at the centre of one of the UK's most ambitious warbird rebuilds

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Claustrophobic prison cells that contain a story worthy of international acclaim, and a vast mountainside complex that held the power to change the world

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: Documentary explaining how a squadron of low-flying bombers freed resistance fighters from Amiens Jail during the Second World War

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: A look at how British-trained Czechs were behind the assassination of Reinhard Heydrich, one of the main architects of the Holocaust

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's journey from Buxton to London continues in Walsall, where he visits a leather business that still supplies the Queen with saddlery. He then explores how the centre of Birmingham has changed since Bradshaw's times, and is taught how to cook a curry by the owner of a leading Indian restaurant. Finally, he heads to the village of Bournville, where he learns how the railway transformed the fortunes of the Cadbury brothers

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues the final leg of his journey, meeting a survivor of the Coventry Blitz and exploring how the city has been rebuilt since the 1940 bombardment. Reaching Aylesbury in Buckinghamshire, he stops overnight at Hartwell House and visits the area's last pure-breed duck farmer, before heading to Watford in Hertfordshire to learn about Operation Pied Piper

programme.title: David Jason: Planes, Trains and Automobiles
programme.desc: The actor takes a vintage school bus trip, rides a San Francisco cable car, meets a legendary motorcycle club and takes to the air in a vintage military helicopter

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The disappearance of an illicit tin of pineapple chunks leaves the inmates horrified that there is a thief in their midst - and for a while, no one is above suspicion. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber is feeling dejected after his romantic fortunes take a turn for the worse, so Fletch tries to revive his friend's flagging spirits. However, his cunning plan has one potential drawback - it involves his own daughter. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher and the rest of Slade's inmates take advantage of the relaxed atmosphere - and of Barrowclough - with Mackay away on holiday. Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale star

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright hatches a plan to get into Nurse Gladys's bedroom by forcing a reluctant Granville to masquerade as a burglar, so the miserly shopkeeper can come to her rescue. Ronnie Barker and Lynda Baron star

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Roy Clarke's comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason. Tight-fisted shopkeeper Arkwright purchases fire-damaged stock, only to find all the tins have lost their labels

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville tries to persuade Arkwright to buy a van, as he is fed up with having to struggle doing the rounds with the shop's outdated delivery bike. He is pleasantly surprised when his skinflint uncle agrees - until he takes the vehicle out on the road. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Jackie Stewart: The Flying Scot
programme.desc: Portrait of the former racing driver, winner of three Formula One World Championships and 27 Grands Prix. The programme chronicles his early career and subsequent success as an internationally respected businessman in charge of his own Formula One team. With archive footage and contributions by friends and colleagues, including Niki Lauda, Emerson Fittipaldi, Sean Connery, Murray Walker and Edsel Ford, as well as Ken Tyrrell's last-ever interview

Plus, Walter Scott's walking cane, and two cricket bats dating back to the 18th century

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The naturalist investigates whether animals and plants have developed an awareness of mathematics, including bamboos that flower exactly the same around the world. Plus, the story of a German horse named Hans that was declared a mathematical genius, but all was not as it seemed

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist investigates two shells that have proved to be winners in evolution - the bird's egg shell and the hard shell of the tortoise. The ostrich egg is so strong it is possible for it to take a person's weight without breaking - so how does a chick manage break out of such a fortress? The evolution of the tortoise shell was for a long time a mystery, and this bony box offers a lot more than just protection

programme.desc: A team of engineers fight to return a veteran Westland Wessex helicopter to the skies - 30 years after its last flight

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches London, where he continues to discover what remains of George Bradshaw's Britain. Arriving at St Pancras, he learns about the station's troubled history and £800million makeover, before travelling along the world's first underground line from King's Cross to Farringdon. The trip comes to an end at the Houses of Parliament, where he climbs the clock tower and meets the men responsible for keeping Big Ben working

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on another journey around the nation with a copy of George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook to discover how the railways have affected people and find out what remains of Bradshaw's Britain. He begins by exploring Brighton's Victorian aquarium, the underground quarries of Godstone, Surrey, and the wonders of the Crystal Palace in suburban south-east London

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The captain of HMS Queen Elizabeth deliberately seeks out the worst possible weather so he can test how the F35 jets cope under extreme conditions - and gets his wish when the aircraft carrier ends up in the path of a hurricane. After consulting with his officers, he resolves to brave the elements and test the jets to the brink of destruction. The crew also faces the challenge of refueling the vessel at sea - one of the hardest tasks outside of going into battle

programme.title: Jim Clark: The Quiet Champion

programme.desc: The suspicious death of Elizabethan noblewoman Amy Dudley, the wife of Robert Dudley, the favourite and alleged lover of Queen Elizabeth I and the man the monarch is said to have wanted to marry - if only he had been available. programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The evolutionary documentary concludes, as David Attenborough profiles the Siamese fighting fish, so aggressive it will fight its own reflection until it is exhausted. He also looks at the kangaroo - once pitted against humans in the boxing ring - which has a kick so strong it can kill

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores nature's evolutionary anomalies and examines two creatures whose stretched features have given them an edge in the natural world. The chameleon has an extra-long tongue that helps it catch prey and the giraffe's neck is so long it is easily able to reach the tops of trees for food

programme.title: Warbird Workshop
programme.desc: Aircraft restoration expert Mark Masters and his son James rebuild a 1943 US Army Aeronca Defender liaison plane for its German owner

programme.desc: A marooned ironclad structure off the coast of England that hides a dramatic story of war and resilience, and a facility in the heart of America that helped tear apart a nation

programme.desc: Stories of people who came close to losing their lives during the Second World War, beginning with tales about the Chindits, the army's elite jungle troops division who beat the Japanese in a daring mission

programme.desc: The hunt for Gestapo head Klaus Barbie and death camp guard John Demjanjuk, dubbed 'The Butcher of Lyon' and 'Ivan the Terrible' respectively, which lasted into the 1980s

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next leg of his journey, learning about the London Necropolis Railway, a funeral service that transported coffins from Waterloo to Surrey's Brookwood Cemetery from 1854 to 1941. He also discovers how London's West End became a popular shopping destination in the 19th century and examines the changing fortunes of the city's docks

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The third leg of Michael Portillo's journey from Brighton to Cromer begins in the London borough of Enfield, where he visits the Royal Small Arms Factory, the largest machine shop in Europe in the Victorian era. He then discovers the influence of trains on horse racing in Newmarket, and heads to Cambridge to find out why it could be considered the birthplace of football

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Within a dense Polish woodland sits a forgotten complex with an explosive history. Also, grand coastal defences in Ghana reveal a tortured past

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Zak and Alex face a four-day mission to deliver a pair of classic steam locomotives for an Autumn Steam Gala at the Mid Hants Railway in Hampshire

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Account of Operation Barbarossa, Germany's plan to invade the Soviet Union in 1941, revealing the massive human and financial cost endured by the Soviet people, who fought a bloody and lonely war with little support from the Allies. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Frasier

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin makes friends with reclusive author TH Houghton, to the considerable dismay of Niles and Frasier, who are frustrated that their own attempts to strike up an acquaintance have failed dismally. Comedy, guest starring Robert Prosky (Hill Street Blues), with Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Denver, Colorado, where he meets the stubborn owner of Italian restaurant Pantaleone's, who appears to be firmly stuck in the past. Pete maintains he makes the best pizza in town - based on a review from the 1980s - but the fiery chef's own taste test proves the opposite. With Pete's wife Paulette and son Josh threatening to leave, can Gordon get through to him and save the business?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Mariam and Nasser, from the West Midlands, find a family holiday home they can eventually retire to in the countryside of Murcia, south-east Spain. The couple used to live near Madrid and desperately miss the Spanish sunshine and way of life, especially the outdoor cooking. Laura has five sizzling hot properties to show them for their £70,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Phil and Caroline Mansell from Wales have been holidaying together in the La Marina Alta region of Spain's Costa Blanca for years. Phil, who suffers from severe arthritis, finds the climate helps his symptoms so they are selling their home back in the UK and are planning a full-time move. Ben Hillman shows them five properties for their £100,000 budget, but will any of them be their dream home?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Le Bistro in Lighthouse Point, Florida, where the stubborn chef and owner has lost his passion for cooking. The visiting expert hopes to help, but his criticisms are not well received, causing tempers to flare

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: The Morgan Motor Company provides unrestricted access to the factory floors, opening its doors to reveal how its £90,000 Plus Six sports car is made, with 170 craftspeople using classic coach-building techniques

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Denise van Outen and her partner Eddie, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv, Zoe Ball and her son Woody Cook, Martin Kemp and son Roman, Maureen Lipman and Gyles Brandreth, Johnny Vegas and his PA Bev, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Emilia Fox and Joanna David, Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford, Laura Whitmore and Iain Stirling, Rylan Clark-Neal and his mum Linda, Georgia Taylor and Sunetra Sarker and Susanna Reid and Rob Rinder critique programmes including Blue Planet II, Unsolved Mysteries, It Chapter Two, Antiques Roadshow, Basic Instinct and news reports on the coronavirus

programme.title: Celebrity First Dates
programme.desc: Pop star Samuel Preston shot to fame when he fell in love with Chantelle Houghton inside the Big Brother house. He wonders whether lightning will strike again on his date with South African Nichole. Gogglebox star and TV presenter Scarlett Moffatt is hoping that martial arts fighter Tommy will prove to be full of banter and happy to see her 'without her fake tan on'. Entrepreneur Jo Wood seeks someone who shares her love of a good time, while Britain's Got Talent winner Ashleigh Butler hopes fellow dog-lover Elliot will live up to her - and Pudsey's - expectations

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A look back at some of Noel Fitzpatrick's most memorable cases including 18-month-old chihuahua Darcy, who suffered a crushed pelvis after being hit by a car, rendering her unable to walk. X-rays revealed at least seven fractures, meaning Noel had to operate on a microscopic scale. The programme also features three-legged cat Jersey, whose sole back leg was taking so much strain that he needed knee surgery, and deerhound Willow, suffering from a suspected broken neck after a horrific accident

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Bolton-based bricklayer Paul Rimmer, who has spent his career restoring and renovating Victorian terrace houses, sets his sights on constructing a state-of-the-art home on a budget of £350,000. However, instead of using the construction materials he has spent the past 40 years mastering, he has opted to build his new home from wood. Roping in his wife Carol, daughter Abby and a selection of favoured workmates, Paul and his team begin work on the ultra-modern, five-bedroom build, and Kevin McCloud reveals how extreme weather, exhaustion, and rapidly dwindling funds place the entire project in jeopardy

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Spanish architect Jaime and his wife Mimi as they embark on a mission to convert a Grade II listed folly into a family home in Buckinghamshire. Giving themselves a wildly optimistic six-month deadline to finish before the birth of their second child, it's not long before the couple hit trouble. Jaime puts all his energies and creativity into the project, using innovative 3D mapping to squeeze living spaces into the tower, but it proves to be an exhausting struggle

programme.title: The Real EastEnders
programme.desc: Filmed in the summer of 2019 by first-time director Ashley Francis-Roy, this documentary presents the world as seen through the eyes of children growing up on council estates on the Isle of Dogs. Brendan is a sixth-generation Islander who's lived with his nan Lynne since he was placed in care when he was four, while 12-year-old Leslie wants to be an electrician, as well as a part-time rapper and gamer - but his mum Tina worries about him hanging out with boys who seek respect and easy money

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Creative Brighton couple Emily and Peter Harold have been holidaying on the Spanish island of Mallorca for 14 years, and are determined to buy a second home there. They're looking for somewhere to relax and soak up the sun and culture on offer in their favourite spot of Port de Soller. Laura Hamilton shows them five apartments for their £250,000 budge

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads to Paris to find out why baguettes go stale so much faster than sliced loaves of bread. Matt Tebbutt is in Italy to investigate why buffalo mozzarella is much more expensive than that from cows and Briony May Williams discovers the surprising secret to the making of mushy peas

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: PR manager Emmy Webster kicks off a week of dinner parties in Yeovil, Somerset, where she hopes her locally sourced produce will impress her guests - self-proclaimed 'country bumpkin' Mel Dunford, used-car salesman Andy Neil, radio broadcaster Steve Carpenter and businessman Alan Bunter - and make her an early contender for the £1,000 prize. However, she is caught out when her hollandaise sauce is found to have been bought from a shop

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Steve Carpenter hosts the second dinner party in Yeovil, Somerset, hoping his country-style cooking and carefully sourced ingredients will win him the £1,000 cash prize. However, the entertainer lets his focus slip when he allows an unwanted ingredient onto one plate

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hosting the third dinner party in Yeovil is diminutive fork-lift truck driver Mel Dunford. With an exotic menu - including duck vindaloo, which is her own invention - Mel hopes to prove her doubters wrong. And it's not just her surprise culinary skills that wow her guests - she also breaks into song, leaving everyone astounded

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businessman Alan Bunter hosts the fourth dinner party in the Yeovil area of Somerset, hoping that his enthusiasm for all things Japanese will help propel him into the lead. However, car salesman Andy Neil and DJ Steve Carpenter fail to be influenced by the calm atmosphere and spend the evening arguing. As the ladies at the table struggle to get a word in, the menu of rotten beans, tofu cheesecake and soggy noodles fails to impress

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Last to host in Yeovil is 50-year-old used-car dealer Andy Neil, who is supremely confident that his fine dining experience, along with the 'beautiful' surroundings of his home, will bag him the £1,000 prize. Six amuse-bouches for his starter are a visual and culinary treat, but his constant questions and need for feedback and praise threaten to put off his fellow diners. He also has to contend with local radio personality Steve Carpenter jockeying for position as top dog of the group, and fussy eater Mel Dunford, who is not afraid to tell him exactly what she thinks of his fancy food

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies is on Spain's Costa Blanca house-hunting around the vibrant resort of Villamartin with Amanda and Steve, who want to buy a holiday home where they can spend the winter months golfing, socialising and soaking up the sun. Danni has been given a budget of £85,000, with which she finds five properties that she thinks fits the bill. Will Amanda and Steve love one of them sufficiently to buy it?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun

programme.title: Frasier

programme.desc: Daphne is dumped by her boyfriend and causes bemusement in the Crane household by immediately embarking on a new relationship - with a man who looks, dresses, talks and acts exactly like Niles. Comedy, starring Jane Leeves and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Arvada, Colorado, where a husband and wife have allowed their restaurant to fall into disrepair. Randy's hoarding is out of control, while Alexa, who runs the kitchen, has a lot to learn. To make matters worse, the Old Neighborhood is one of the filthiest eateries the fiery chef has ever seen, and only one of the biggest transformations of the series can save the business

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The semi-finals have arrived, with four teams battling for a place in the final, with the challenges including five hours to create a magical Charlotte Russe display, while at the same time preparing all of the elements needed to perform a spectacular, World of Magic-inspired piece of gourmet theatre, known as live plating. At the end of the challenge, the teams have just three minutes to transform their table into a gastronomic work of illusion and theatre, under the tough gaze of judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden

programme.title: Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri (2017)
programme.desc: A woman pressures the police into continuing the investigation into her daughter's murder. Martin McDonagh's Bafta and Oscar-winning crime drama, starring Frances McDormand, Sam Rockwell and Woody Harrelson

programme.title: Catching a Killer
programme.desc: When elderly teacher Peter Farquhar was found dead at home in the village of Maids Moreton in 2015, it was sad and unexpected, but no one thought it suspicious. But 18 months later, when his neighbour Ann Moore-Martin also fell ill and died, the police decided to look again. They found that Ben Field, a would-be vicar and academic, had recently become part of both of their lives and benefited from their wills. This programme shows that by delving back in time through friends' and loved ones' memories of Peter and Ben's relationship, as well as Peter's diary, the police discovered a story of love and betrayal

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: On the waterways of Mylor Creek, southern Cornwall, lifelong friends and mariners Jess Harris and Charlotte Whyte welcome diners aboard their new custom-built floating restaurant. On the southern edge of Dartmoor, sheep farmers Jess and Russell Steer prepare for their first trip to Devon's historic Widecombe fair. Near Exmoor, Cyril Cole is determined to give back to the land he once farmed

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Denise Greenacre has retired and is determined to buy a home in Spain. Ben Hillman shows Denise and her daughter Rebecca five properties around Torrevieja on the Costa Blanca for her £80,000 budget

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two sets of house-hunters looking for homes in the historic city of York. Kirstie helps first-time buyers Tom and Timna, who met at university, married soon after and are now expecting their first child. With the lease on their rental almost up, time is of the essence find them their family home. Phil is searching for Yorkshire-born Rich and Kirsty, who have been living it up in London but want to move back up North to be closer to their family - and to start their own

programme.title: Flirty Dancing
programme.desc: Ashley Banjo teaches singletons from Paignton, Taunton, Stratford-upon-Avon and London half of a fully choreographed routine, bringing them together only on the day they perform Ashley Banjo tries to bring singletons together through the art of dance, teaching strangers one half of a fully choreographed routine, and only bringing them together on the day they perform. The potential couples this week are classroom leader James from Paignton and Taunton children's home manager Jessica, and children's occupational therapist Stephanie and graphic designer Baz, from Stratford-upon-Avon and London respectively. Will sparks fly on the dance floor?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hotel consultant Oli Delaunoy kicks off the week of dinner parties in Cheltenham, Gloucestershire, and hopes that his scallop starter and a meaty main course will impress his guests - events-company director Zak Roby, administrator Kim Thomas, house husband Paddy Castledine and nurse Jan Blomer - and win him the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nurse Jan Blomer hosts the second dinner party in Cheltenham, Gloucestershire, hoping her menu of chicken with sun-dried tomatoes and home-made ice-cream will win her the £1,000 cash prize. Paddy and Zak's rivalry keeps the evening lively and loud, but the host reclaims the spotlight with a surprising revelation

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Events director and bodybuilding enthusiast Zak Roby hosts the third evening in Cheltenham and hopes his polite nature will help him scoop the £1,000 prize. However, a series of confusing party games alongside tricks and posturing at the dinner table mean Zak's quest is far from straightforward

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Australian admin assistant Kimberley Thomas hosts the fourth dinner party in Cheltenham and hopes that an evening of sophistication will win her the £1,000 prize. However, despite her best attempts to create a dignified atmosphere, it does not take long for Paddy Castledine to begin telling rude stories

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Paddy Castledine hosts the final dinner party in Cheltenham, and hopes that his simple menu of locally sourced produce will impress his guests. As the evening draws on, admin assistant Kimberley Thomas tells the host what she really thinks of him, and alpha male Oli Delaunoy is brought to tears, before the winner of the £1,000 cash prize is revealed

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton shows five apartments to Nottingham-based social worker Sheila, who wants to buy a holiday home on Spain's Costa Blanca

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Dick and Liz Bradley's Bryn Derwen B&B in Rhayader, Powys. The breakfast is hot - but things quickly chill when grievances are mentioned

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Sharon and Shaun help a Leamington Spa couple find a countryside villa with a pool, while Sophie and Damon try to find a retirement property for Eileen and Martin from Lincoln

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Lilith and Frasier are forced to spend time together while trying to convince the headmaster of an exclusive Boston school that their precocious son Frederick would be the ideal pupil. Comedy, guest starring Bebe Neuwirth

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin feel indebted to Frasier and buys him an expensive work of art to repay his hospitality - but the gesture is not appreciated. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Queens, New York, where he meets a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband-and-wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, while their daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle and at her wits' end

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Following a recent health scare, beauty consultant Marcelle and coach company owner Graeme are seizing the moment and about to buy a holiday home in western Malaga for stress-free holidays together. They'd love to find an apartment near the buzzing town of Benalmádena with a communal pool and private outdoor space and have given Jasmine Harman a budget of £120,000 to find the perfect property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Denise Greenacre has retired and is determined to buy a home in Spain. Ben Hillman shows Denise and her daughter Rebecca five properties around Torrevieja on the Costa Blanca for her £80,000 budget

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay goes back to two restaurants - Giuseppi's and Sante La Brea - to check in with the owners he helped in the past and find out whether his advice has been put to good use. Will he be pleased with their progress? Plus, an update on Finn McCool's from season one

programme.title: Jimmy's Forest
programme.desc: As autumn arrives in the Norfolk forest, Jimmy Doherty gives a pair of his friends the task of foraging for fruit and nuts when they visit his tree house. The presenter also learns about the methods kestrels use to hunt, meets a man who claims he is able to speak to owls, makes ink from oak galls, and discovers the extraordinary natural properties of deer antlers

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: On the waterways of Mylor Creek, southern Cornwall, lifelong friends and mariners Jess Harris and Charlotte Whyte welcome diners aboard their new custom-built floating restaurant. On the southern edge of Dartmoor, sheep farmers Jess and Russell Steer prepare for their first trip to Devon's historic Widecombe fair. Near Exmoor, Cyril Cole is determined to give back to the land he once farmed

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: Mental health nurse Jim counsels people who are struggling with self-isolation, while paramedics Karina and James respond to a call from a man who claims to be armed and is threatening suicide. Despite losing his mother to Covid-19, paramedic Richard continues working on the frontline while also managing his anxiety about taking the virus home to his wife, whose complex health issues put her at high risk

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients arriving at King's College Hospital in London include 26-year-old Nicholas, whose jaw has been fractured and dislocated in an unprovoked attack, and Tyrell, 17, who needs to have a blackened toenail removed following a football injury. Meanwhile, Ho has brought his grandmother Amoui to the ward after finding her collapsed in her flat

programme.title: The Real EastEnders
programme.desc: Filmed in the summer of 2019 by first-time director Ashley Francis-Roy, this documentary presents the world as seen through the eyes of children growing up on council estates on the Isle of Dogs. Brendan is a sixth-generation Islander who's lived with his nan Lynne since he was placed in care when he was four, while 12-year-old Leslie wants to be an electrician, as well as a part-time rapper and gamer - but his mum Tina worries about him hanging out with boys who seek respect and easy money

programme.title: How to Build

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Elaine and Nicos Nicolaou seek to return to their Greek roots by buying a property in Kefalonia, and Jasmine Harman seeks out five properties in the island's southern region that fall within the couple's £60,000 budget

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke meets an old friend in Norfolk who is planning to turn an old sewage works into a unique chill-out space for his family, complete with a self-supporting dome roof and a circular carp pond. A Midlands father and daughter work together on salvaging a dilapidated childhood treehouse with a budget of just of £300, George and Will find unusual inspiration as they continue building their own treehouse, and in Japan, George visits a breath-taking chapel made of intertwining concrete ribbons

As usual, the identities of the owners of these loveable rogues and their beautiful homes remains concealed - at least for a while. Narrated by Kevin McCloud

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Claire and Simon seek eviction specialist Chris's help to evict a man claiming to be renting their old family home. Meanwhile, Hamza hopes to get his council flat back after he tried to help a neighbour's friend by letting them stay for a week, but ended up being shut out when they refused to leave. Plus, a man is almost £30,000 in debt and in danger of repossession after his tenants stopped paying the rent

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Experienced enforcement agent Stewart and trainee Iain head to Blackpool to chase down a builder owing nearly £3,500 to an unhappy customer, while father and son Del and Dael are in Essex to collect over £2,500 owed to a frozen food company. In south London, Del and Brian attempt to recover nearly £10,000 from a debtor who acted as guarantor for a friend's rental agreement, ending in a bizarre stand-off with the debtor's daughter

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben try to drill through the locks on a property to evict the residents, only to find to their surprise that the door has been modified with special reinforced metal. Elsewhere, Delroy and Brian try to reclaim money a garage owes to a customer and are shocked by the condition of the vehicle in question, and a woman who has acted as guarantor for her sister's tenancy does not realise the extent of her responsibilities

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement agents evict a large group of squatters barricaded into a disused nursery in London, and have to resort to smashing their way in as they refuse to leave. Meanwhile, agents in Bristol try to recover over £10,000 owed by a plumbing business to a supplier, and emotions run high when they attempt to evict a tenant who pleads her innocence in West London

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: An episode combining new and unforgettable past cases. Later in south London, the agents deal with a violent debtor who threatens them with a concealed weapon, and in Liverpool, they are met with lies and aggression from a mechanic who owes nearly £3,000

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jon Moon and his dog Kanto are hot on the heels of some burglary suspects, while spot-checks by the unit on the A1 turn up a carrier bag full of cash. Jason Baxter rushes to the scene of an accident where the driver was thrown clear of the car, only for another crash to take place - this time involving a line of vehicles

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A foul-mouthed pensioner pushes Barry's patience to the limit, Sarah gives a bad-boy biker an early birthday present and Jim's Audi S3 is outclassed by a car 30 years its senior. Plus, Mike is left open-mouthed when he encounters a drink-driver who is three times over the limit

programme.title: Neighbours

programme.desc: Police officers monitor events as thousands of protesters wearing V for Vendetta masks march to the Houses of Parliament

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A one-off edition of the documentary in which journalist Nick Wallis reveals how CCTV and technological advances are helping to ensure convictions, focusing on criminals in London involved in robberies, thefts, drug crimes, street brawls and weapons offences. In Soho, police officers monitor the activity of the area's 100,000 nightly visitors from a CCTV control room in an underground bunker, while in Lambeth the air support unit is called in when an armed gang are seen rampaging through the streets

programme.title: Fare Dodgers: At War with the Law
programme.desc: A prolific offender is landed with an eyewatering bill to square off three years of illegal travel, while the police must step in when one suspect is found in possession of a banned substance. At one of London's busiest stations, vigilant inspectors Scott and Michelle have success intercepting repeat offenders, but will they catch a notorious passenger who the station staff report comes through their station each day?

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Cleaning duo Jon and Brennan take on some of Britain's filthiest and most cluttered homes. In three days, the pair bring the grubbiest homes back to life

programme.title: Skin A&E

Dr Natalia Spierings treats 42-year-old Shaneef's shoulder lipoma, Dr Adil Sheraz tackles a large head cyst, and Dr Dev Shah comes under fire over his lack of music knowledge. Plus, psoriasis-sufferer John feels he's exhausted all the options for treatment, but can Dr Emma McMullen offer him a ray of hope?

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: Return of the documentary looking at how people buy, sell and market sex, hearing from an array of individuals working within Britain's multimillion-pound industry. programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: The documentary looks at a hidden world of pensioners who are selling sex when most people of their age are planning their funerals. Those featured include Norm, an 85-year-old former priest now enjoying a new career as a porn star, and 72-year-old transgender escort Pippa Jane

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: A disparate group of young sex workers reveal what life is really like trying to make a living as escorts, cammers and sex entrepreneurs. programme.desc: The agents chase almost £3,000 owed by Indian takeaway owners, but when the staff deny any knowledge of the debt, the agents threaten to seize kitchen equipment. In east London they face a race against time to recover more than £8,000 from an electrical services company, and in Cheshire the enforcement agents chase nearly £4,000 of debt owed by a mobile phone shop

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The enforcement officers visit a former professional footballer who owes £4,500 in unpaid legal fees, and in London the officers chase up a £4,000 debt owed by a car dealer. Agents Gary Brown and Cona Jackson are involved in a serious altercation with a young man who owes almost £11,000, who threatens the officers with a knife when they start listing goods to seize to offset the debt

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The officers chase a debt of almost £2,000 in unpaid parking fines, and later encounter the extraordinary case of a debt of £80,000 owed by a hotel owner to his ex-girlfriend. However, as they begin to search the property, they call upon police back-up as the agents fear the debtor may have access to firearms. Elsewhere, two officers head to a car garage in Manchester where the owner owes £2,000 after losing a court case over a faulty vehicle

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: PC Barry Peach hunts for a man wanted for assault, officers are hot on the heels of a suspected drug dealer, a drunken driver tries every trick in the book to avoid giving a breath sample and dog-handler Jon Peacock has a late-night encounter with two unusual law-breakers

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jim intercepts a car containing cannabis, a late-night reveller talks himself into trouble and Sgt Baxter has his work cut out when confronted by a dishonest driver. Plus, young drivers get a crash course in how to deal with skids at a training session run by the unit at RAF Scampton

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Mackenzie is thrilled over the gender confirmation surgery, and Hendrix is stoked when Harlow organises a party for him at the Backpackers, but something goes seriously wrong

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: John receives the news that he will finally be discharged, and Jasmine's behaviour comes to the forefront when she posts to the mother's forum referring to Grace as her baby

programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: Life on the dole in Scotland, including aspiring pop stars Zedd and Saffire, who live in Portobello, Edinburgh and receive £17,000 a year in benefits. After a failed attempt to start her own business in Australia, Kit is back in her home town of Wishaw, North Lanarkshire, to sign on and stay with friends, while Stirling resident Kirsty is desperate for work and is convinced her weight and mild learning disability are stopping her from getting a job. The 30-year-old's stay-at-home parents Rosie and Desmond believe that discrimination is to blame

programme.title: Britain's Council House Millionaires
programme.desc: Documentary about some of the nation's wealthiest buy-to-let landlords, who have made a fortune by buying up former council homes, and renting them out to those in need. Among those featured is a Manchester property magnate who buys properties in the city's less desirable areas and rents them to students at up to £400 a month for a room, a London estate agent who only deals in former council properties, and a Kent man who grew up in a council house, and recognises the quality of the construction

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: The specialist clinic opens its doors to patients with acne, psoriasis and cysts.

programme.title: The Others (2001)
programme.desc: A woman awaits the return of her husband from the Second World War while struggling to care for her son and daughter. Both children suffer a violent reaction when exposed to daylight, forcing her to keep much of their isolated home on the island of Jersey shrouded in gloom. When the pair claim to see strangers in the house, their terrified mother begins to suspect that the souls of the dead are stalking the halls. Period supernatural thriller, starring Nicole Kidman, Fionnula Flanagan, Alakina Mann, James Bentley and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Rookie Blue
programme.desc: A routine bust in a drugs den soon becomes Detective Nash's first solo homicide case. Starring Missy Peregrym

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy and Borgia prosecute a famous basketball player accused of murdering a fan who had been harassing him for some time. They discover the star's fingerprints in the victim's apartment, but the evidence linking him to the crime scene is dismissed as inadmissible because the detectives cut corners to find a match on the police database. Starring Sam Waterston and Annie Parisse

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green believe the fatal stabbing of psychologist Tracy Conley may be linked to confidential client information on her computer. They learn one of the files on her hard drive has been tampered with since her death - information that would have ensured a known rapist spends another five years behind bars. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: When an 11-year-old girl is found suffocated to death in Central Park, Briscoe and Green discover her adoptive mother's attempts to discipline the child may have inadvertently caused the tragedy. However, the case is thrown into confusion when a post-mortem reveals she may actually have died of an allergic reaction. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A nurse with a social agenda is arrested on suspicion of performing illegal sterilisations on troubled teenagers without their knowledge - a policy that has resulted in the death of one patient. As McCoy and Borgia prepare their case against her, they discover former ADA Paul Robinette is planning to act for the defence

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate a spate of murders and find a recurring pattern, leading them to believe they have a serial killer on their hands. They soon find a suspect, who admits to killing 15 women - but he then puts a disconcerting spin on things by claiming his attorney knows more about the crimes than they realise

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green are called in to investigate the murder of a notorious bounty hunter. Piecing together his final movements, they turn their attentions to a meeting between the victim and an ambitious journalist, which may be able to shed light on what happened

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A deep-sea diver is murdered on the job, and his co-workers all become suspects. However, when they return to the surface, they must remain in a high-tech decompression chamber for four days. With each of their suspects confined within a tank, the NCIS team must complete an unorthodox investigation from the outside. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the Secretary of the Navy, learns her daughter has been kidnapped, and the NCIS crew teams up with the FBI to track the case, determine a motive and bring the abducted girl home. Meanwhile, McGee's childhood friend, NCIS Special Agent Valerie Page, arrives in town to assist with advanced tactics training. Naval crime drama, guest starring Leslie Hope and Christina Chang

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A day spent working on a cut-and-dried case leaves the team free to spend some time away from work, and while McGee and Delilah argue over the importance of keeping shop-talk to a minimum, DiNozzo ends up boring his dinner date with crime-solving tales. However, the agents' personal plans are all interrupted as, one-by-one, they realise errors have been made in closing their latest investigation. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An American couple are attacked in Iraq, in the very same spot a group of US Special Ops snipers were ambushed six months earlier. Determined to get to the bottom of the case, Gibbs is forced to speak to Aaron Davis, the only survivor of the first assault, who is now struggling through PTSD at the Walter Reed Medical Centre. The 300th episode of the military crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: A woman is attacked in her own home, and the man who once stalked her becomes the prime suspect. Guest starring Chris Bauer (True Blood)

programme.title: Rookie Blue
programme.desc: Andy and Chloe are attacked by an unseen shooter when they respond to a routine 911 call. When more shots are fired at a subsequent emergency call, the officers realise that 15 Division is being targeted by someone out for revenge. The clock is ticking as the squad tries to serve and protect, while running down suspects. Meanwhile, Oliver comes face to face with the sniper in a life-changing moment

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A car chase through Manhattan leaves one man dead and a teenager injured, leading Fontana and Green to discover the victim was being followed after massacring a hunting party. As the man who led the chase is publicly lauded for his brave efforts, McCoy and Borgia make their case that his poor decisions only endangered other lives. Starring Sam Waterston and Annie Parisse

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A high-school student is found dead in an apartment building, and Briscoe and Green suspect an improper website may have played a part in her murder. As McCoy and Southerlyn investigate the case further, they realise the victim's best friend, who was reluctant to come forward as a witness, may provide the answers. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach, Jesse L Martin and Elisabeth Rohm

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of an investment analyst whose dealings with a bankrupt company left many clients penniless. Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin star

programme.desc: Fontana and Green track down a disturbed woman who has not only murdered a young mother and stolen her five-day-old son, but also managed to convince her own husband that the baby is really theirs. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A vial containing a sample of the Sars virus is stolen during a carjacking, leaving Briscoe and Green spearheading a wide-scale hunt for a man dubbed patient zero - the first victim to have contracted the illness. They finally home in on a female reporter who recently ended a long-term relationship with a virologist

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An actress is found stabbed to death in an award-winning songwriter's apartment, and police believe the musician knows more than he is letting on. Clues lead them to the victim's therapy group as they try to discover who would profit from her death. Starring Jerry Orbach and Sam Waterston

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team looks into a raid on a naval base, and Dr Cyril Taft - the surgeon who saved Gibbs' life - experiences the thrill of assisting on an investigation. Assigned to treat Gibbs' suspect, Taft manages to uncover evidence that may prove crucial to solving the case. Meanwhile, DiNozzo pays his old flame Jeanne Benoit a visit as he chases a lead involving doctors in Sudan. Guest starring John Cryer and Scottie Thompson

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A day spent working on a cut-and-dried case leaves the team free to spend some time away from work, and while McGee and Delilah argue over the importance of keeping shop-talk to a minimum, DiNozzo ends up boring his dinner date with crime-solving tales. Each woman offering a very different take on the chain of events that led to the murder, and the investigators are forced to decide which of the two is telling the truth. Meanwhile, DiNozzo's father comes to the aid of a homeless woman, who makes a surprising claim about his relationship with her. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS

Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

American crime drama, starring Nathan Fillion and Stana Katic

Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach

Meanwhile, Director Vance and FBI Agent Fornell travel to London as part of the ongoing case involving the escaped British spies

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

First episode of the spin-off drama, starring Mariska Hargitay, Christopher Meloni and Richard Belzer

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The officers are called in to investigate when a successful newspaper columnist falls six storeys to her death, dressed only in her underwear. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.desc: The team investigates a youth hockey team, where bullying of new players has taken a dangerous turn

programme.desc: A dying man hires Thomas to find his estranged brother, who turns out to need help as well, and the case brings back memories of a SEAL team mission that went wrong

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: A look at the 2010 murder of two pensioners in Torquay, who were killed by a moneygrabbing man who tricked his way into their lives

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Grissom, Sara and Greg try to determine how a man came to be crushed to death in a Las Vegas hotel room, following a trail of clues that leads them to a convention for overweight people. Meanwhile, Catherine, Nick and Warrick are called to the outskirts of the city, where a mutilated body has been found - and discover a large sum of money nearby, along with betting slips that suggest a possible motive for the killing. Starring William Petersen

programme.title: Brit Cops
programme.desc: Documentary series following the work of police officers in Lincolnshire as they tackle crime

programme.title: Road Wars

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: The true story of Tim Schrader, who was caught smuggling 10.5kg of heroin out of Bangkok and was sentenced to life in prison, where he became infected with HIV

programme.desc: Documentary series following the work of police forces across Australia as they deal with situations from sieges and major drug busts, to murder investigations

programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The band performs on a space station, but accidentally bring along an alien who paralyses the whole crew

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The pop stars have to rescue X from his old protege, who wants to take over the world

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Tjure's new baby is having trouble sleeping - and cries so loudly that Halvar also gets no sleep the night before an important battle

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Snorre's mother refuses to let the harvest celebration go ahead until her smelly son has cleaned up his act - so Vic tries to trick him into washing

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: A story read by Zara makes Laurence keen to know if unicorns exist

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: Zara finds some interesting items as she hunts for dinosaur relics

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Lotus starts to turn into a bamboo plant after being bitten by a snake, and Tashi and Jake must find a cure before the Baron cuts down the whole bamboo forest

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: A magic carpet sends Jack and Tashi back to prehistoric times. When they return, they discover they have changed history so monkeys now rule the world

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple who have decided to sell their B&B farmhouse in Devon and make their home Down Under in Sydney discover their £400,000 will soon be swallowed up in the highly desirable city. However, their hopes are raised when they find a restored Edwardian country house and an architect-designed property overlooking the harbour on offer at £93,000 under budget

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow experiences what New York was like at the height of the Industrial Revolution. Millions fled to escape the persecution, poverty and famine of 19th-century Europe, only to be confronted by living conditions worse than those they had left behind. He explores the overcrowded slums of Lower Manhattan, which were a breeding ground for cholera and typhoid, and has a go at cooking using clothes dye and floor cleaner - ingredients that were added to meals to disguise the smell of spoiled meat. He also discovers how certain feats of engineering transformed not only the city, but the world

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Luftwaffe chief and designated successor to Hitler, Hermann Goering. The larger-than-life character was renowned for his charisma and flamboyance, but also as one of the most unrepentant and ruthless of all Nazis. Having aimed to use the courtroom as a means of rehabilitating the fascist regime, it was only at a late stage that he was forced to concede defeat. Robert Pugh stars

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams looks at the role - and the unusual odd jobs - of country policemen in the South and South East during the Second World War, when rumours of chicken stealing and illegal pig roasts were fairly common diversions from the privations of rationing

programme.title: Earth Cycle
programme.desc: Simon Parker charts the seasonal cycles of the natural world as he journeys through Scandinavia on a bicycle, wild camping, seeing animal migrations and sampling local food

programme.title: The Rats of Tobruk (1944)
programme.desc: Two tough Australian soldiers fight alongside an intellectual Englishman in the North African campaign. They become involved in a critical siege, and their fearless efforts manage to hold up the German advance for months. Second World War drama, starring Grant Taylor, Peter Finch and Chips Rafferty

programme.title: All Coppers Are (1971)
programme.desc: A young policeman and a small-time crook are both involved with the same girl. Drama, starring Martin Potter, Julia Foster and David Essex

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Featuring inspirational walks from around Britain

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple with a budget of £80,000 fly out to Puerto Vallarta on Mexico's Pacific coast in search of a holiday home they can rent out to fund a trip round the world. The glorious weather and beaches are matched by bargain-priced properties, including a seaview apartment, a family home with separate studio and a coastal penthouse boasting its own pool

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Wartime drama starring Anthony Andrews as young officer Brian Ash who joins the UXB unit during the Blitz the military organisation responsible for defusing unexploded German bombs

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Luftwaffe chief and designated successor to Hitler, Hermann Goering. The larger-than-life character was renowned for his charisma and flamboyance, but also as one of the most unrepentant and ruthless of all Nazis. Having aimed to use the courtroom as a means of rehabilitating the fascist regime, it was only at a late stage that he was forced to concede defeat. Robert Pugh stars

programme.title: How to Stay Young
programme.desc: In the second of two documentaries about beating the ageing process, Angela Rippon and Dr Chris van Tulleken find out what people can do to give their brains a boost. Angela travels to Japan, where she discovers the foods that can help protect your brain, and meets the 100-year-old who is living proof of the importance of diet in keeping the grey matter healthy. In the USA, Chris finds out about a trial in which scientists are hoping to beat Alzheimer's disease by injecting sufferers with the blood of young people

programme.title: Bare Knuckle
programme.desc: Documentary exploring bare knuckle boxing's rise in popularity, its evolution through centuries, legal issues & the fight to give modern day boxers professional recognition

programme.title: Hooligan Escape: The Russian Job (2018)
programme.desc: Five British football fans are arrested while attending the World Cup in Russia. However, their problems get even worse when a corrupt guard hands them over to an old enemy who has been plotting revenge for years. Thriller, starring Ali Bastian and Charlie Wernham

programme.title: Cruel Passion (1977)
programme.desc: A twisted tale of strange desires, perverse pleasures and the ultimate corruption of innocence as told by the Marquis de Sade

programme.title: Love Is the Devil (1998)
programme.desc: The story of George Dyer, a petty criminal who became the model and lover of leading Dublin-born artist Francis Bacon. Hopelessly at odds with Bacon's cynical, sophisticated milieu and bewildered by the painter's cruelty toward him, Dyer sought refuge in drugs and alcohol. British fact-based drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Daniel Craig, Tilda Swinton and Adrian Scarborough

programme.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread (1991)
programme.desc: A wealthy widow travels to Italy to escape the pressure of her in-laws, and promptly falls in love with a poor local. Period drama, based on the novel by EM Forster, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Judy Davis and Rupert Graves
